name: Acid Gator
description: A large alligator surrounded by a cloud of acid.

features.name: Acid Cloud
features.description: When the Gator is spotlighted, all PCs within Melee range must mark an Armor Slot. If they can't mark an Armor Slot, they must mark a HP.

features.name: Tail Swipe
features.description: Make an attack against a target in Very Close range. On a success, deal 2d10+5 physical damage and the target is knocked back to Far range.

features.name: Clamp Jaws
features.description: When the Gator's standard attack causes a target to mark HP, you can mark a Stress to make the target temporarily Grappled. While Grappled, the target is Restrained and must mark a Stress when they make an action roll. Frost Bears are found in arctic regions and radiate cold. They hunt prey that aren’t naturally resistant to their innate cold, such as adventurers.
features.trigger: When the Gator's standard attack causes a target to mark HP

name: Adlet Sled-Raiders
description: Dog-people of snow-hard trails, charging in a spray of grit and breath.

features.name: Horde (2d4+2)
features.description: When the Raiders have marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 2d4+2 physical instead.

features.name: Sledge Charge
features.description: Mark a Stress to sweep in a straight line from Far to Close, making an attack against all in your path; targets you succeed against take 2d6+3 physical and are knocked to Far unless they succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll (14).

features.name: Harness Drag
features.description: When your attack causes a target to mark HP, they become Restrained until they clear a Stress or succeed on a Strength Roll (14).
features.trigger: Your attack causes a target to mark HP

name: Aether Magus
description: A mage draped in heavy robes with a large crow skull where its head should be

features.name: Ice Spike
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target within Far range. On a success, mark a Stress to Restrain the target. A target can make a Strength Roll (10) to end the Condition.

features.name: Flash Freeze
features.description: Mark a Stress and choose a point within Far range. A Very Close area around that point becomes slick with ice. All targets in the area must make a Finesse Reaction Roll (12). Targets who fail take 2d6 physical damage and become Vulnerable until they next act.

features.name: Reflection of Death
features.description: When the Magus takes damage, Spend a Fear to force the attacker to make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, the Magus and the attacker both take half the damage received.
features.trigger: When the Magus takes damage

name: Alkonost
description: A radiant bird with the face of a sorrowful woman, the Alkonost sings of forgotten joys and distant peace. Her song brings blissful silence—but those who listen may never wish to wake.

features.name: Flying
features.description: While flying, the Alkonost gains a +2 bonus to their Difficulty.

features.name: Lullaby of Lost Purpose
features.description: Spend a Fear to cause all targets within Far range to forget their goals and desires. All targets affected replace their Hope Die with a d8 until they roll a success with Hope or their next rest.

features.name: Focused Lament
features.description: When an ally within Close range makes an attack roll, mark a Stress to let the ally make the roll with advantage. When the attack is successful, the target must mark a Stress.

name: Alraune Queen
description: A walking plant covered in rose blooms and dagger-sized thorns. It grows by consuming the blood of its victims.

features.name: Watered By Blood
features.description: The Queen enters the scene with 2d4 blood tokens. For each token the Queen has they gain a +1 bonus to damage. If a target marks HP within Close range of the Queen, she gains another token. If the Queen takes Severe damage, she loses 1d4 tokens.

features.name: See How My Garden Grows
features.description: Spend a Fear to Summon 1d4 Young Alraune that appear within Close range and immediately take the spotlight.

features.name: Feed, My Children
features.description: Spend a Fear to spotlight 1d4+1 allies. Attacks they make while spotlighted in this way deal half damage.

features.name: Thorn Cage
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against a target within Far range. On a success, deal 2d10 physical damage and the target must succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll or become Restrained. If the target is flying they take an additional 1d10 damage (combine the damage). The target can break free with a successful Strength or Finesse Roll.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Queen makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: successful attack against PC

name: Amalgamation
description: An amorphous slime with protruding faces and other body parts bearing an eerie resemblance to their former prey.

features.name: Surround
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, the target takes 2d8 physical damage and the Amalgamation surrounds them. The target is Vulnerable while the Amalgamation surrounds them and they must mark a Stress when they act.

features.name: Sorrow Song
features.description: Mark a Stress to sing a song that affects all targets within Far range. Targets must succeed on a Presence Reaction Roll or lose a Hope and move up to Close range towards the Amalgamation. If they have no Hope, you gain a Fear.

features.name: Discordant Screams
features.description: Spend a Fear to force all targets within Very Close range to make an Instinct Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 2d6+5 direct magic damage. Targets who succeed take half damage.

features.name: Absorb
features.description: Twice per scene, when the Amalgamation moves over a corpse, absorb them to gain their memories and clear a HP or Stress.

name: Amarok, Lone-Howl
description: The great wolf of the Inuit night, hunting alone where the snow sparks under its paws.

features.name: Hunts Alone
features.description: If no other adversary is within Close, your standard attack has advantage and deals +1d6 damage.

features.name: Whiteout Lunge
features.description: Move to Very Close and make a standard attack. On a success, the target must succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll (14) or be knocked prone and Restrained.

features.name: Midnight Howl
features.description: When a PC spends Hope within Far, mark a Stress to force that PC to make an Instinct Reaction Roll (14) or become Vulnerable.
features.trigger: A PC spends Hope within Far

name: Animated Shadow
description: A creature's shadow come to life, with glowing eyes.

features.name: Shadow Form
features.description: The Shadow is resistant to physical damage. If the Shadow is within Very Close range of bright light, they are Vulnerable.

features.name: Slink into Shadow
features.description: Become Hidden until after the Shadow's next attack. Attacks made while Hidden from this feature have advantage.

features.name: Drain Essence
features.description: Mark a Stress to force a target to make a Presence Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target's Presence trait is reduced by 1d4 until they succeed on a Presence Roll or their next rest.

name: Animated Statue
description: A humanoid statue bestowed with limited sentience.

features.name: Hardy
features.description: Reduce incoming damage by 1d6.

features.name: Rubble Toss
features.description: Mark a Stress to grab loose rubble and throw it at a target within Close range. If no item is available, mark a HP to tear off a piece of the Statue. Make an attack against a target. On a success, deal ldl2+4 physical damage.

features.name: Topple
features.description: Spend a Fear to have the Statue knock down a structure within Melee range they could feasibly topple (such as another statue or a load-bearing wall) causing it to collapse. Targets in range of the toppled structure must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take 2d10 physical damage.

name: Antibody
description: A protein that hunts for foreign bodies.

features.name: Anti-Body
features.description: Mark a Stress to transform into an eerie likeness of a PC. They gain access to the PC's current active weapon(s), dealing the same amount of damage as the PC on a successful attack. They also gain access to the effects of two random card in the PC's loadout. To use a card feature, when relevant spend or gain Fear instead of Hope. The Antibody can take the spotlight a second time this GM turn.

name: Apprentice Assassin
description: Un jeune apprenti désireux de faire ses preuves.

features.name: Minion (6)
features.description: L’Assassin est vaincu quand il subit n’importe quels dégâts. Pour chaque tranche de 6 dégâts qu’un PJ inflige à l’Assassin, vainquez un Minion supplémentaire à portée que l’attaque toucherait.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Choisissez une cible et placez sous le projecteur tous les Apprentis Assassins à Portée Proche d’elle. Ces Minions se déplacent à Portée de Mêlée de la cible et font un jet d’attaque partagé. En cas de réussite, ils infligent 4 dégâts physiques chacun. Combinez ces dégâts.

name: Archer Squadron
description: Un groupe d’archers entraînés portant des arcs massifs.

features.name: Horde (1d6+3)
features.description: Quand l’Escouade a coché la moitié ou plus de ses PV, son attaque standard inflige 1d6+3 dégâts physiques à la place.

features.name: Focused Volley
features.description: Ciblez un point à Portée Loin. Faites une attaque avec avantage contre toutes les cibles à Portée Proche de ce point. Les cibles touchées subissent 1d10+4 dégâts physiques.

features.name: Suppressing Fire
features.description: Ciblez un point à Portée Loin. Jusqu’au prochain jet avec Peur, une créature qui se déplace à Portée Proche de ce point doit faire un jet de Réaction d’Agilité. En cas d’échec, elle subit 2d6+3 dégâts physiques. En cas de réussite, elle subit la moitié des dégâts.

name: Ashborn Marshal
description: A towering knight in heavy plate wielding a spiked flail.

features.name: Ramp Up
features.description: You must spend a Fear to spotlight the Marshal. While spotlighted, they can make their standard attack against all targets within range.

features.name: Powerful Blows
features.description: A target hit by the Marshal is knocked back to Close range.

features.name: Vengeful
features.description: If an ally within Very Close range marks its last HP, you gain a Fear.

name: Assassin Poisoner
description: Un vaurien rusé spécialisé dans les poisons et les embuscades.

features.name: Grindletooth Venom
features.description: Les cibles qui cochent des PV suite aux attaques de l’Assassin sont Vulnérables jusqu’à ce qu’elles libèrent un PV.

features.name: Out of Nowhere
features.description: L’Assassin a l’avantage sur ses attaques s’il est Caché.

features.name: Fumigation
features.description: Lancez une bombe fumigène qui remplit l’air à Portée Proche de fumée, Étourdissant toutes les cibles dans cette zone. Les cibles Étourdies ont un désavantage sur leur prochain jet d’action, puis retirent la condition.

name: Avian Aeronaut
description: A bird-like humanoid who is deadly with a bow and arrow.

features.name: Net Arrow
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack using an arrow with a weighted net in an extradimensional space within the tip. On a success, the target is Restrained in the net and flying targets become grounded. The target can break free and end the condition with a successful Finesse Roll (12).

features.name: Bomb Arrow
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack using an arrow with a black powder keg in an extradimensional space within the tip. On a success, the attack deals 2d12 direct magic damage.

features.name: Evasive Maneuver
features.description: Mark a Stress to increase the Difficulty of the Avian by +3 against an attack from Close range or further. Avian Divebomber The Divebomber obtains javelins and other objects from an extradimensional space and can fight in melee or at range. Avian Wind-Dancer A Wind-Dancer is a master of manipulating the winds. Several Wind-Dancers can combine abilities to change the weather.

name: Avian Divebomber
description: A bird-like humanoid who bravely swoops into combat.

features.name: Kamikaze
features.description: When the Avian moves to Close range and makes a successful standard attack against a target within Melee range, you can mark a HP to deal 2d12+4 physical damage instead of their standard damage.

features.name: Flyby
features.description: While flying, the Avian moves up to Close range after making a standard attack.

features.name: Pocket Weight
features.description: Once per scene, spend a Fear to take a heavy weight such as an anvil from an extradimensional space and drop it on a target. The target must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take 2d20 direct physical damage.

name: Avian Wind-Dancer
description: A bird-like humanoid who can control air currents.

features.name: Whirlwind
features.description: Conjure a large gust of wind. All targets within Close range must succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll or be knocked back to Far range.

features.name: Precise Strike
features.description: Mark a Stress and make a standard attack against a target within Far range.

features.name: Chaotic Winds
features.description: Spend a Fear to spin up winds and summon a Minor Chaos Elemental (SRD) within Close range with 4 HR

features.name: Deflect projectile
features.description: Mark a Stress to increase the Difficulty of the Avian or an ally within Very Close range by +1 against a ranged attack. B Basilisks Basilisks are creatures distinguished by their stripy blue hides and unsettling yellow eyes. Basilisks come in terrestrial, aquatic, and winged varieties, and each has a deadly gaze which transforms the skin of its prey. Basilisk The standard land-dwelling Basilisk has many legs, a spiked hide, and uses its gaze to turn creatures to stone. The Basilisk proved tricky to draw. Fortunately, Eryn unpetrifiedme afterwards. -	Archibald

name: Bacteria Swarm
description: A swarm of unicellular microorganisms.

features.name: Multiply
features.description: Mark a Stress to gain an additional 2 HP, even if this would take it beyond the Bacteria's HP maximum.

features.name: Toxic Burst
features.description: When the Bacteria marks HP, deal 2d8 magic damage to all targets within Melee range.
features.trigger: When the Bacteria marks HP

features.name: Bacterial Resistance
features.description: When the Bacteria marks HP, you can spend a Fear to become permanently resistant to that type of damage. Inner Demon Inner Demons take over the brain and manifest dark and troubling thoughts.
features.trigger: When the Bacteria marks HP

name: Banshee
description: Known in Irish as Bean Sí or Bean Sidhe, this ghostly omen of death lets loose a wailing cry that heralds the end. Drifting unseen through mist and moonlight, the banshee is ever present at the final breath of the doomed.

features.name: Ghost
features.description: The Banshee has resistance to physical damage. Mark a Stress to move up to Close range through solid objects.

features.name: Omen's Keening
features.description: Spend a Fear to make a soul-piercing keening scream. All targets within Close range must make an Instinct or Presence Reaction Roll. If they succeed, they must mark a Stress. If they fail, they are temporarily Staggered. While Staggered, all attack rolls they make are at disadvantage, unless they spend a Hope before they make the attack roll. A target is Staggered until the next turn.

features.name: Death's Rebuke
features.description: When the Banshee takes damage from a creature within Close range, she can mark a Stress to make a standard attack against that creature. If she succeeds, the target is hurled back to Far range and temporarily Vulnerable.
features.trigger: Takes damage from a creature within Close range

name: Baobhan Sìth, Dance of Needles
description: Perfume like crushed ivy, steps like falling leaves. Her shadow pirouettes a heartbeat out of time.

features.name: White Ribbon
features.description: Place a Ribbon circle (Very Close radius) within Close. Creatures who enter or start in the circle must succeed on a Presence Reaction Roll (14) or step toward the dancer (up to Very Close) and become Charmed. Charmed creatures have disadvantage on all Presence Reaction Rolls until they mark 2 Stress to break free.

features.name: Hollow Waltz
features.description: While inside the Ribbon circle, Baobhan Sìth is semi-corporeal (resistance to physical damage).

features.name: Sanguine Courtesy
features.description: When she causes a target within Very Close to mark HP, clear 1 Stress and the target must spend a Hope or become Vulnerable until their next action.
features.trigger: She causes a target within Very Close to mark HP

name: Barghest
description: A barghest (also barguest) is a massive, jet-black hound with eyes like burning coals, its appearance a harbinger of death. It haunts lonely crossroads and shadowed lanes, vanishing into the dark as swiftly as it strikes.

features.name: Death Omen
features.description: The first time a PC sees a Barghest, it must make an Instinct or Presence Reaction Roll. On a failure, the PC loses a Hope and must mark a Stress. This effect can only occur once per PC per scene.

features.name: Rip and Tear
features.description: When the Barghest makes a successful standard attack, you can mark a Stress to temporarily Restrain the target and force them to mark 2 Stress.
features.trigger: Makes a successful standard attack

features.name: Drag into Darkness
features.description: Spend a Fear to disappear from where you are and reappear inside another shadow within Far range. When you have a target Restrained by Rip and Tear, you can take the target with you. The Barghest and the Restrained target are Hidden from other creatures.

name: Basilisk
description: A creature with a blue striped body and a deadly gaze.

features.name: Easy Pickings
features.description: Targets of the Basilisk's attacks must state if they are looking at the Basilisk. The Basilisk has advantage against targets who avoid looking at the Basilisk.

features.name: Tail Slam
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success the target takes 2d6+l physical damage and is knocked back to Close range. A target with an active petrification countdown takes double damage.

features.name: Petrifying Gaze
features.description: When the Basilisk takes damage from an attacker within Very Close range, or makes a standard attack against a target that is looking at them, you can spend a Fear to force the target to make an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, they begin to turn to stone, marking a HP and starting a Petrification Countdown (4). The countdown ticks down when the Basilisk is attacked. When it triggers, the target must make a death move. If the Basilisk is defeated, all petrification countdowns end.
features.trigger: When the Basilisk takes damage from an attacker within Very Close range

features.name: Spiky Hide
features.description: When an attack from Melee range misses the Basilisk, the attacker takes 1d6+3 direct physical damage. Aquatic Basilisk An Aquatic Basilisk has a gaze that can calcify creatures, which they can then dissolve with their acidic breath. Winged Basilisk The Winged Basilisk uses their gaze to vaporize their prey, turning them into digestible and buoyant solid cloud.
features.trigger: When an attack from Melee range misses the Basilisk

name: Battle Box
description: Un constructe en forme de cube avec une rune différente sur chacune de ses six faces.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: La Boîte peut être mise sous le projecteur jusqu’à deux fois par tour du MJ. Dépensez une Peur comme d’habitude pour la placer sous le projecteur.

features.name: Randomized Tactics
features.description: Lancez un d6. 1 : Rayon de Mana — attaque Loin, 2d10+2 mag. 2 : Jets de Feu — attaque tous Proche, 2d8 phy. 3 : Piétinement — attaque tous Proche, 1d6+5 phy + Vulnérable. 4 : Gaz Étourdissant — jet de Réaction de Finesse ou cochez 3 Stress. 5 : Claque Étourdissante — jet de Réaction de Force ou Vulnérable. 6 : Gémissement Psionique — jet de Réaction de Présence ou 2d4+9 dégâts mag. directs.

features.name: Overcharge
features.description: Avant de lancer les dégâts de l’attaque de la Boîte, ajoutez un d6 au jet de dégâts. De plus, vous gagnez une Peur.

features.name: Death Quake
features.description: Quand la Boîte coche son dernier PV, la magie qui l’anime se rompt en une explosion. Toutes les cibles à Portée Proche doivent réussir un jet de Réaction d’Instinct ou subir 2d8+1 dégâts magiques.
features.trigger: Quand la boîte coche son dernier pv, la magie qui l’anime se rompt en une explosion

name: Battle Pyramid
description: A pyramid-shaped construct with a different rune on each of their four sides.

features.name: Randomized Tactics
features.description: Mark a Stress and roll a d4. The Pyramid uses the corresponding move. 1.	Death Glare. Eyes appear on each face of the Pyramid. All targets within Close range must make a Presence Reaction Roll. Targets who fail must mark 2 Stress and become Vulnerable. Targets who succeed must mark a Stress. 2.	Acid Spray. The Pyramid shoots into the air, spinning and releasing a spray of acid. Make an attack against all targets within Very Close range. Targets the Pyramid succeeds against take 2d4 magic damage and must mark an Armor Slot without receiving its benefits (they can still use armor to reduce the damage). If they can't mark an Armor Slot, they must mark an additional HP. 3.	Caltrops. The Pyramid releases caltrops in the area up to Close range. All PCs moving through caltrops must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take 2d4+3 direct physical damage. 4.	Knockout Gas. The Pyramid sprays out a purple gas. All creatures within Very Close range must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or fall Asleep until they take damage or at the end of the scene.

features.name: Death Quake
features.description: When the Pyramid marks their last HP, the magic powering them ruptures in an explosion of force. All targets within Close range must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or take 2d6+l magic damage. Beasts Beastly adversaries are dangerous due to their large size, strength in numbers, and/or ability to be trained for combat. Standard Beasts are described here, but they can easily be reflavored into new monsters. You can roll a d10 one or more times on the table below to do so. The same can be applied to other Beasts in this book or in the SRD. Reflavored Beast Prompts Roll	Narrative Flavor 1	Head: Humanoid head, oversized head, long neck, gaping maw 2	Eyes: Extra eyes, glowing eyes 3	Mouth: Monstrous teeth, tusks 4	Appearance: Spotted, striped, unusual color, luminescent, skeletal 5	Texture: Hairy, scaled, glass, slimy 6	Aura: Cute, dark, ghostly, elemental 7	Noise or Smell: Makes chittering or singing noises, foul smelling 8	Features: Antlers, floppy ears, horns, spines 9	Limbs: Extra arms, extra legs, elongated limbs, bladed limbs, shell 10	Adaptations: Prehensile tail, fins, wings, fur, humps Beast Companion Beasts that are trained as pets - such as a loyal canine companion or bear cub - defend the party and can aid in tracking with their keen senses. Elephant Elephants can be found in tropical forests and grasslands. Woolly Mammoths might be found in arctic regions.
features.trigger: When the Pyramid marks their last HP

name: Bearcupine
description: A bear with spiky quills on their back.

features.name: Beastly Power
features.description: When you initially roll damage, if you roll the maximum value on a die, also roll a new die of that type and add it to the total.

features.name: Spine
features.description: Mark a Stress to pull a quill javelin from the Bearcupine's body and throw it at a target. Make an attack against a target within Close range, dealing 2d8+5 physical damage on a success.

features.name: Spiky Hide
features.description: When an attack from within Melee range doesn't succeed against the Bearcupine, the attacker takes 2d6 direct physical damage. Griffon A Griffon is half-eagle and half-lion. Griffons make excellent flying mounts for travel and combat.
features.trigger: When an attack from within Melee range doesn't succeed against the Bearcupine

name: Blabbering Mound
description: A horrid mass of eyes, mouths, and formless flesh. Staring in all directions, its countless maws yammer ceaselessly.

features.name: Crawling Confusion
features.description: The Mouther can only move within Very Close range as their normal movement.

features.name: Gibbering
features.description: Spend a Fearand target all PCs within Close range. They must make an Instinct Reaction Roll or mark a Stress and become Disoriented. When aDisorientedtarget takes the spotlight, the GM suggests a course of action. The player can take that action or mark a Stress. A PC clearsDisorientedwhen they clear Stress or when they take the spotlight and there are no Blabbering Mounds within a Far range.

features.name: Blinding Spittle
features.description: Mark a Stressand pick a point within Close. All targets within Very Close range of that point must make an Agility Reaction Roll or mark a Stress and becomeVulnerableuntil they next take the spotlight.

features.name: Warped Ground
features.description: When a PC moves into or within Very Close range of the Mouther, they must make a Finesse Reaction Roll or mark a Stress.
features.trigger: When a PC moves into or within Very Close range of the Mouther

name: Black Annis
description: A dreadful blue-skinned hag with iron claws and a single blazing eye, Black Annis stalks the night from her cave in the hills. She snatches children and wanderers alike, drags them into her lair, and wears their flayed skins as trophies of terror.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: Black Annis can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Rend and Crush
features.description: If a target damaged by Black Annis doesn't mark an Armor Slot to reduce the damage, they must mark a Stress.

features.name: Rend the Flesh
features.description: Mark a Stress to lash out and attack all targets in Very Close range at advantage. Any you are successful against take 2d8+4 phy damage and are Vulnerable until they heal at least one Hit Point.

features.name: Terrible Howl
features.description: Spend a Fear to unleash Black Annis' dreadful scream. All enemies within Close range must make an Instinct or Knowledge Reaction Roll. On a failure, a target loses 1d4 Hope. On a success, a target marks 1 Stress.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When Black Annis makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: Makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Blasphemous Angel Evelyar
description: Her holy mission slandered and her compassion punished

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: Evelyar can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight her.

features.name: Unholy Aura
features.description: When Evelyar takes damage, reduce it by the number of Profane Disciples within Close range. When a PC marks their last HP within Close range of Evelyar, she heals a HP and clears a Stress.

features.name: Arc of Judgement
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against all targets within Very Close range. On a success, they take2d8+5physical damage.

features.name: The Flock’s Call
features.description: Mark a Stressto spotlight1d4+1allies. Attacks they make during this spotlight deal half damage.

features.name: Look Into The Void
features.description: Mark a Stressto have a PC within Very Close range make an Insight or Presence Reaction Roll. On a failure, they mark a Stress, they becomeVulnerableuntil they roll a success with Hope, and Evelyar heals 2 HP.

features.name: Blasphemous Choir
features.description: Countdown (Loop 1d8). When Evelyar is in the spotlight for the first time, activate the countdown. When it triggers, a choir of discordant voices covers an area within Far range of Evelyar. When a target marks HP from an attack by Evelyar within the dark song, all PCs within Far range of the target lose a Hope. This song can be ended by dealing Severe damage to Evelyar, after which the countdown restarts.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Fane Warden makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

name: Blood Mage: Abomination
description: A vaguely-humanoid soup of magical blood and malice, pseudopods forming weapons.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Abomination can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Crimson Whirlwind
features.description: Spend a Fearto make a Bloody Scythe attack against all targets within Close range. On a success, targets hit by the attack must also mark a Stress.

features.name: Sanguine Ballista
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Far range. On a success, the target takes2d12+5mag damage.

features.name: Blood Siphon
features.description: When a PC marks 2 or more HP from one of the Abomination’s Attacks, mark a Stressas the Abomination siphons their blood. The abomination clears 1 HP and you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When a PC marks 2 or more HP from one of the Abomination’s Attacks

features.name: Unstable Form
features.description: When the Abomination marks 2 or more HP from an attack within Close range, the attack causes a bloody detonation, forcing the attacker to make an Agility Reaction Roll or mark a Stress and becomeVulnerableuntil their next roll with Hope.
features.trigger: When the Abomination marks 2 or more HP from an attack within Close range

name: Blood Mage: Malefactor
description: A corrupted mage with blood-red robes seeking slaughter to gain more power.

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: The Malefactor can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Hemokinetic Burst
features.description: Mark a Stressto pick a point within Far range. All targets within Very Close of that point must make a Strength Reaction Roll or be knocked prone and made temporarilyVulnerable until they next take the spotlight.

features.name: Sanguine Apotheosis (Phase Change)
features.description: When Malefactor first spots a hostile target, activate a Countdown (4) that only advances when a PC rolls with Fear. If triggered, replace Malefactor with Abomination, who immediately takes the spotlight.
features.trigger: When Malefactor first spots a hostile target

features.name: I Will Not Be Denied!
features.description: When Malefactor marks their last HP, you mayspend any number of Fearto reduce the Sanguine Apotheosis countdown by the same amount. If that would trigger the countdown, replace Malefactor with Abomination as per Sanguine Apotheosis.
features.trigger: When Malefactor marks their last HP

name: Blood Ooze
description: A moving mound of blood-colored slime.

features.name: Drain Strength
features.description: Mark a Stress to force a target with marked HP to make a Strength Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target's Strength trait is reduced by 1d4 until they succeed on a Strength Roll or their next rest.

features.name: Blood Absorption
features.description: Two times per scene, when the Ooze moves over a corpse or pool of blood, consume the blood to clear a HP or a Stress.

features.name: Split
features.description: When the Ooze has 3 or more HP marked, you can spend a Fear to split them into two Tiny Blood Oozes (with no marked HP or Stress). Immediately spotlight both of them.
features.trigger: When the Ooze has 3 or more HP marked

name: Bog Ravager
description: A ribbet warrior with spear in hand and a barbed tongue

features.name: Barbed Tongue
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against a target within Close range. On a success, the target takes 2d10+2 physical damage and the Ravager moves to a point within Melee of the target.

features.name: Run Through
features.description: If the Ravager causes a target to mark HP, they also lose a Hope. If the target cannot lose a Hope, they mark an additional HP. The dense jungles spread out into the valley and grow without regard for those who once lived alongside it or the things they built. Now, legions of zombies, tribal ribbets and corrupted fungrils haunt the shadows. Nature seeks to do more than overtake, it actively hungers for the blood of those who would dare step foot within, growing twisted guardians from the bramble and vines.
features.trigger: If the Ravager causes a target to mark HP

name: Bog Sprite
description: A faerie that has become a shadow of its former self

features.name: Withering Presence
features.description: Creatures within Very Close range have disadvantage on action rolls to cast spells.

features.name: Umbral Incursion
features.description: Mark a Stress and target a group within Far range. All targets must succeed on a Presence Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 2d10+3 magic damage are Weakened. While Weakened, a target’s thresholds are halved until they next mark HP, then clear the condition.

features.name: Blink
features.description: When the Sprite is targeted by an action roll, roll a d6. On a 5 or greater, the Sprite is unaffected by the effects of the roll.
features.trigger: When the Sprite is targeted by an action roll

name: Boggart
description: An apparition that haunts homes with violent pranks and theft of possessions.

features.name: Harmed By ...
features.description: The Boggart is immune to physical and magical damage unless they are made Vulnerable. Only a specific material can damage them as determined by the GM. Some examples might be salt, iron, fire, or appeasement. In such cases, the Boggart marks all Stress and becomes Vulnerable.

features.name: Get Out!
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against all targets within Very Close range. Targets the Boggart succeeds against take no damage, but become Vulnerable until the end of the scene.

name: Bookwyrm
description: A dragon librarian that guards over a library.

features.name: Shapeshift
features.description: Mark a Stress to change between the Bookwyrm's Drakona form and Dragon form before taking an action. When they change to the Dragon form for the first time, you gain a Fear. Relentless (2): The Dragon can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Hush
features.description: Mark a number of Stress to quieten that many targets within Close range of the Bookwyrm. The targets must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or become temporarily Silenced while within Close range of the Bookwyrm. While Silenced, they are unable to cast spells or speak aloud.

features.name: Animate Books
features.description: Mark a Stress to create a Swarm of Animated Objects made of flying books within Close range.

features.name: Dizzying Breath
features.description: Spend a Fear to breathe a cloud of gas that covers the area in front of the Bookwyrm up to Close range. All targets must make an Instinct Reaction Roll. Targets who fail are temporarily Dizzied, and must mark a Stress to move. The Dizzying Breath also douses flames. Bookwyrm A Bookwyrm is a dragon with a hoard of books. Bookwyrms build great libraries, which they guard over to ensure visitors give the books sufficient respect. Rushfal’s library books were organized by a neat “Dragon DecimalSystem”. - Archibald Chaos Dragon Chaos Dragons are dragons with a lair in the Chaos Realm and have been subsequently warped by it, with unnatural proportions and abilities that defy logic. Drake Drakes are unintelligent slug-like draconic creatures that gather near a dragon’s lair and scavenge from whatever the dragon leaves behind.

name: Brambleguard
description: A doll-like creature of the jungle made of bark and vines

features.name: Thorn Whip
features.description: Mark a Stress to force a target within Close range to make a Strength Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target gains a Thorn token, is pulled into Melee range and marks a Stress. All Thorn tokens can be removed by succeeding on a Strength Roll (13 + the number of thorn tokens).

features.name: Thornweaver’s Shield
features.description: If the Brambleguard takes damage from an attacker that has at least one thorn token, mark a Stress to reduce the damage by half.
features.trigger: If the Brambleguard takes damage from an attacker that has at least one thorn token

name: Bridge Troll
description: A large humanoid with gangly limbs who extracts a toll to grant passage across a bridge.

features.name: Thick Hide
features.description: The Troll is resistant to physical damage.

features.name: Regeneration
features.description: If the Troll hasn't taken physical damage since they last had the spotlight, you can mark a Stress to clear a HP.

features.name: Over the Edge
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, spend a Fear to throw the target in an arc up to Close range. If the fall isn't prevented during the PCs' next action, the target falls from the bridge and takes 2d20 physical damage when they land.

name: Bukwus, Bone-Beggar
description: A Kwakwaka'wakw wild man of the cedar gloom, offering ghost food in a palm of wind.

features.name: Food of the Dead
features.description: Offer unseen delicacies to all targets within Close. Each target chooses: partake (mark a Stress and gain advantage on their next action, but you count them as In a Wake: your attacks against them deal an additional +1d6) or refuse and become Vulnerable until they mark a Stress.

features.name: Between Cedar Shadows
features.description: While within forest, you have resistance to physical damage and advantage on Instinct Reactions.

features.name: Hollow Invitation
features.description: When a PC has no ally within Close, mark a Stress to move adjacent and become Hidden.
features.trigger: A PC has no ally within Close

name: Bunyip
description: A frog-like creature that stands three stories tall. Its immense jaws can swallow a person whole.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Bunyip can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Bellowing Roar
features.description: Spend 2 Fear to let out a reverberating call. All targets in the scene must succeed on a Strength or Instinct Reaction Roll. Targets who fail become Hopeless. While Hopeless a target cannot gain Hope until the Bunyip is defeated.

features.name: Tentacle Grab
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target. On a success, mark a Stress to pull the target into Melee range of the Bunyip. The target is temporarily Restrained.

features.name: Tentacle Swing
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against all targets in front of the Bunyip at Very Close range. Targets the Bunyip succeeds against take 2d8+6 physical damage and you gain a Fear for each.

features.name: Devour
features.description: Force a target the Bunyip has Restrained to make a Strength Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target takes 2d8 direct physical damage and is Devoured. While Devoured, the target is also Hidden and must mark a Stress to make an action roll. When the Bunyip takes Severe damage, all Devoured targets are freed and the condition is cleared.

features.name: Digest
features.description: Spend a Fear to cause all Devoured targets to take 2d8 direct physical damage.

name: Centaur Archer
description: A half-horse half-human creature armed with bow and arrow.

features.name: Double Strike
features.description: Mark a Stress to make a standard attack against two targets within Close range.

features.name: Written in the Stars
features.description: Once per scene, when the Centaur fails an action roll, you can mark a Stress to reroll. Centaur Destrier Centaur Destriers fight in close quarters, trapping the creatures they hunt in nets before moving in for the kill. Centaur Druid Centaur Druids lead strikes against urban populations that the Centaur considers to be harming the surrounding nature.

name: Centaur Destrier
description: A half-horse half-human creature that charges at their foes.

features.name: Charge
features.description: Mark a Stress to move to Far range and make a standard attack with advantage against a target. On a success, the target must also mark a Stress.

features.name: Lasso
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success the target is bound by rope held by the Centaur. The target moves with the Centaur and is Restrained and Vulnerable until they break free with a successful Finesse Roll.

features.name: Written in the Stars
features.description: Once per scene, when the Centaur fails an action roll, you can mark a Stress to reroll.

name: Centaur Druid
description: A half-horse half-human creature who vigorously defends nature.

features.name: Plant Growth
features.description: Mark a Stress to grow a Thornbush within Close range.

features.name: Warhorn
features.description: Spend 2 Fear to spotlight 1d4 allies. Attacks they make while spotlighted in this way deal half damage.

features.name: Wrath of Nature
features.description: Countdown (Loop 1d6). When the Centaur is in the spotlight in an urban space for the first time, activate the countdown. It ticks down when a PC rolls with Fear. When it triggers, you gain a Fear and a building collapses from thick vines growing up the side of the building tearing it down. All targets affected by the building's collapse must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 2d20 direct physical damage. Targets who succeed take half damage.
features.trigger: Countdown (Loop 1d6)

features.name: Written in the Stars
features.description: Once per scene, when the Centaur fails an action roll, you can mark a Stress to reroll. Cherax A Cherax is a crustacean warped by eldritch magic in the deep abyss of the ocean. Taking in this magic has made a Cherax sentient but completely insane, unable to fully comprehend knowledge of the universe a crustacean should never have to learn. The Cherax kept muttering about “the Drowned One”, “soul anchors”, “finding a vessel”, and other nonsensical mumbo-jumbo. - Eryn

name: Chaos Skull
description: Un crâne humanoïde flottant animé par une magie scintillante.

features.name: Levitation
features.description: Le Crâne lévite à quelques pieds du sol et ne peut pas être Entravé.

features.name: Wards
features.description: Le Crâne est résistant aux dégâts magiques.

features.name: Magic Burst
features.description: Faites une attaque contre toutes les cibles à Portée Proche. Les cibles touchées subissent 2d6+4 dégâts magiques.

features.name: Siphon Magic
features.description: Faites une attaque contre un PJ avec un trait de Spellcast à Portée Très Proche. En cas de réussite, la cible coche 1d4 Stress et le Crâne retire autant de Stress. En cas de réussite, le Crâne peut immédiatement être remis sous le projecteur.

name: Cherax
description: A crustacean warped by the deep abyss.

features.name: Spreading Madness
features.description: Targets who mark HP from the Cherax's attacks must also mark a Stress. If they have marked all Stress, the target becomes Maddened until they clear all marked Stress. While Maddened they can only take short rest downtime moves during a long rest and they develop a flaw such as a compulsion, obsession, or phobia.

features.name: Double Strike
features.description: Mark a Stress to make a standard attack against two targets within Melee range.

features.name: Psychic Visions
features.description: Spend a Fear to project nightmares to all targets within Very Close range. Targets must make a Presence Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 3d6 magic damage. Targets who succeed take half damage. Chosen Chosen are warriors appointed by a God to enact their will and oversee tasks within their domain.

name: Clank Beast
description: A clank with the form of a quadrupedal beast, often used as a mount.

features.name: Ejector Seat
features.description: Mark a Stress to launch a mounted rider at a target within Close range. The rider can make a standard attack against the target, with advantage on the attack roll. The target must also mark a Stress.

features.name: Pulse Cannon
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against a target within Close range, dealing 2d6+6 magic damage on a success and knocking the target back to Far range. dank Juggernaut A Clank Juggernaut is an unstoppable creation dispatched to deal with troublemakers. Clank Petard A Clank Petard has a compartment containing an explosive device that they can use to destroy a gate or wall.

name: Clay Golem
description: A clay construct etched with runes.

features.name: Rune of Protection
features.description: While the Golem has marked 3 or fewer HP, they have resistance to magic damage.

features.name: Mold Clay
features.description: Mark a Stress to gain advantage on an action roll by shaping the end of the Golem's limb into a form such as a hammer or key.

features.name: Runic Glitch
features.description: When the Golem marks 2 or more HP, they can glitch. Move to Close range and deal 2d10+5 physical damage to all targets in their path, knocking the targets back to Close range. Ice Golem Steel Golem Ice Golems are created in cold climates and shipped to warm climates, where they are magically protected from the heat and can preserve food by creating a cold storage.
features.trigger: When the Golem marks 2 or more HP

name: Clear Ooze
description: A moving mound of transparent slime.

features.name: Sticky
features.description: The Clear Ooze can climb across walls and ceilings.

features.name: Hard to See
features.description: Attacks made against the Ooze from Close range or further have disadvantage.

features.name: Globule
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against a target within Close range. On a success, the target takes 2d6+4 magic damage and is temporarily Restrained.

features.name: Split
features.description: When the Ooze has 3 or more HP marked, you can spend a Fear to split them into two Tiny Clear Oozes (with no marked HP or Stress). Immediately spotlight both of them.
features.trigger: When the Ooze has 3 or more HP marked

name: Clurichaun, Toastmaster of Chaos
description: Wine-wet whiskers, a corkscrew in the belt, a laugh that sets chairs on their hind legs.
motives: Start a fight with a cheer
Leave unpaid tabs in your name

features.name: A Cursed Toast!
features.description: Offer a toast to all within Close. Each target must choose: drink (mark a Stress and gain advantage on their next attack) or abstain (gain disadvantage on their next action). You gain a Fear for every two who drink.

features.name: Rolling Barrel
features.description: Mark a Stress to set a barrel rolling from Close to Far in a straight line, knocking back all in its path to Very Far unless they succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll (14).

features.name: Last Call
features.description: When reduced to 1 HP, you may spend a Fear to shatter every unattended bottle within Close; a fog of spirits fills the area (Lightly Obscured), creatures entering must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or become temporarily Vulnerable.
features.trigger: Reduced to 1 HP

name: Cockatrice
description: A bipedal hybrid lizard-bird with a barbed tail, flightless wings, and a confounding stare.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Cockatrice can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Terrifying
features.description: When the Cockatrice makes a successful attack, all PCs within Far range lose a Hope and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Barbed Tail Lash
features.description: Spend a Fearto make an attack against all targets within Very Close range. Targets the attack succeeds against suffer2d12phy damage and must mark an armor slot without gaining its benefit (they may still use armor to reduce the damage).

features.name: Eyes of the Void
features.description: Mark a Stressto force all targets in front of the Cockatrice within a Close range to make an Instinct Reaction Roll or become Disoriented. ADisorientedtarget gains disadvantage on action rolls. A target clears Disorientedwhen they succeed on an action roll or at the end of the scene.

features.name: Turn Inside Out
features.description: When the Cockatrice succeeds on an attack against aDisorientedtarget, the target must mark a Stress and the Cockatrice clears a Stress. Cockatrice
features.trigger: When the Cockatrice succeeds on an attack against aDisorientedtarget

name: Conscript
description: Un civil mal entraîné enrôlé de force pour la guerre.

features.name: Minion (6)
features.description: Le Conscrit est vaincu quand il subit n’importe quels dégâts. Pour chaque tranche de 6 dégâts qu’un PJ inflige au Conscrit, vainquez un Minion supplémentaire à portée que l’attaque toucherait.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Choisissez une cible et placez sous le projecteur tous les Conscrits à Portée Proche d’elle. Ces Minions se déplacent à Portée de Mêlée de la cible et font un jet d’attaque partagé. En cas de réussite, ils infligent 6 dégâts physiques chacun. Combinez ces dégâts.

name: Corrupted Fane Warden
description: The once beloved protector of the weald now stews in

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Fane Guardian can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Glimmer Within
features.description: A flame of ancient magic still burns within the Fane Warden. Whenever a PC would cause the Fane Warden to heal HP or Stress, start aProgress Countdown (4), reducing it by 1 each additional time it would be healed one HP or Stress. When this countdown would count below 1, the Warden is restored.

features.name: Overwhelming Arc
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against every target within Very Close range. On a success, they take2d10+8 physical damage and must make a Strength Reaction Roll (15). On a failure, they are pushed away to Far range of their location.

features.name: Vine Spear
features.description: Spend a Fear to have a target within Far range make a Strength Reaction Roll (15). On a failure, they are pulled to anywhere within Very Close range of the Fane Warden and the Fane Warden immediately makes an attack against them.

features.name: Take Root
features.description: Mark a StresstoRootthe Fane Warden in place. Fane Warden is RestrainedwhileRooted, and can end this effect instead of moving while they are spotlighted. WhileRooted, Fane Warden has resistance to physical damage.

features.name: Righteous Vengeance
features.description: When the Fane Warden marks 2 or more HP from an attack within Very Close range,mark a Stress to make a standard attack against the attacker. On a success, the Fane Warden deals2d10+8physical damage and the target is pushed to Far range.

name: Corrupted Treant
description: A flowering treant with scythe-like arms controlled by a corrupted dryad

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Treant can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Heart Thorns
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target. On a success, Spend a Fear to cause the target to lose a Hope and make a Reaction Roll with their highest trait. On a failure, they cannot gain Hope until the end of the scene.

features.name: Rock Launch
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against a group within Far range. Targets the Treant succeeds against take 3d6 physical damage and have the Vulnerable condition until they next act.

features.name: Wide Swing
features.description: Spend a Fear to make a standard attack against all targets in front of the Treant within Very Close range.

features.name: Spore Scream
features.description: When the Treant marks its last HP, it wails as it ejects a cloud of spores. All targets within Close range must make an Instinct Reaction Roll. Targets who fail lose 3 Hope. If they don’t have enough Hope, they lose all Hope and must mark 1 HP.
features.trigger: When the Treant marks its last HP

name: Courtesan
description: Un manipulateur accompli et maître des arts sociaux.

features.name: Searing Glance
features.description: Quand un PJ à Portée Proche fait un jet de Présence, lancez un regard vers l’issue. En cas d’échec de la cible, elle doit cocher 2 Stress et est Vulnérable jusqu’à la fin de la scène ou jusqu’à ce qu’elle réussisse une action sociale contre le Courtisan. En cas de réussite de la cible, elle doit cocher un Stress.
features.trigger: Quand un pj à portée proche fait un jet de présence, lancez un regard vers l’issue

name: Cù Sìth, Hill's Shadow
description: Green-furred as moss, paws that make no sound, breath like heather smoke. It barks thrice when doom comes.

features.name: Three Barks (Countdown 3)
features.description: When first spotlighted, begin a Barks countdown that ticks down whenever a PC rolls with Fear. When it triggers, all targets within Close must succeed on a Presence Reaction Roll (14) or lose a Hope and become Vulnerable until they clear a Stress.

features.name: Heather Lurch
features.description: Erupt from cover; move to Very Close and make a standard attack. On a success, also knock the target back to Close.

features.name: Grass-Wisp
features.description: When hit by a ranged attack, vanish into a flank—move to Close to the attacker and the attacker becomes Dizzied. Dizzied targets have disadvantage on their next action roll, then clear the condition.
features.trigger: Hit by a ranged attack

name: Cult Adept
description: Un mage expérimenté maniant l’ombre et la peur.

features.name: Enervating Blast
features.description: Faites une attaque standard contre une cible à portée. En cas de réussite, la cible doit cocher un Stress.

features.name: Shroud of the Fallen
features.description: Enveloppez un allié à Portée Proche d’un linceul de Protection jusqu’à ce que l’Adepte coche son dernier PV. Tant que Protégée, la cible a une résistance à tous les dégâts.

features.name: Shadow Shackles
features.description: Choisissez un point à Portée Loin. Toutes les cibles à Portée Proche de ce point sont Entravées par des chaînes de fumée jusqu’à ce qu’elles se libèrent avec un jet de Force ou d’Instinct réussi. Une cible Entravée par cette feature doit dépenser un Espoir pour faire un jet d’action.

features.name: Fear Is Fuel
features.description: Deux fois par scène, quand un PJ obtient un échec avec Peur, retirez un Stress.
features.trigger: Quand un pj obtient un échec avec peur, retirez un stress

name: Cult Fang
description: Un tueur professionnel devenu cultiste.

features.name: Shadow's Embrace
features.description: Le Croc peut grimper et marcher sur les surfaces verticales. Cochez un Stress pour se déplacer d’une ombre à une autre à Portée Loin.

features.name: Pick Off the Straggler
features.description: Forcez une cible à Portée de Mêlée à faire un jet de Réaction d’Instinct. En cas d’échec, la cible doit cocher 2 Stress et est téléportée avec le Croc vers une ombre à Portée Loin, la rendant temporairement Vulnérable. En cas de réussite, la cible doit cocher un Stress.

name: Cult Initiate
description: Un cultiste de bas rang en robes simples, avide de pouvoir.

features.name: Minion (6)
features.description: L’Initié est vaincu quand il subit n’importe quels dégâts. Pour chaque tranche de 6 dégâts qu’un PJ inflige à l’Initié, vainquez un Minion supplémentaire à portée que l’attaque toucherait.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Choisissez une cible et placez sous le projecteur tous les Initiés du Culte à Portée Proche d’elle. Ces Minions se déplacent à Portée de Mêlée de la cible et font un jet d’attaque partagé. En cas de réussite, ils infligent 5 dégâts physiques chacun. Combinez ces dégâts.

name: Damask Ambusher
description: A hardened cutthroat and thief who hunts for the Queens.

features.name: Backstab
features.description: When the Ambusher succeeds on a standard attack that has advantage, they deal2d10+8physical damage instead of their standard damage.

features.name: Cloaked
features.description: BecomeHiddenuntil after the Ambusher’s next attack. Attacks made whileHiddenfrom this feature have advantage.

name: Damask Archer Squad
description: Ranged raiders of the Damask Queens.

features.name: Horde (1d6+3)
features.description: When the Squad has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals1d6+3physical damage instead.

features.name: Focused Volley
features.description: Spend a Fear to target a point within Far range. Make an attack with advantage against all targets within Close range of that point. Any you succeed against take1d10+4physical damage.

features.name: Suppressing Fire
features.description: Mark a Stressto target a point within Far range. Until the next roll with Fear, a creature who moves within Close range of that point must make an Agility Reaction Roll (14). On a failure, they take2d6+3physical damage. On a success, they take half.

name: Damask Marauder Band
description: A crew of cackling killers relishing in chaos and plunder.

features.name: Horde (2d4+2)
features.description: When the Band has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals2d4+2physical damage instead.

features.name: Sadistic Laughter
features.description: When a target of the Band’s attack marks 2 or more HP, you can mark a Stress to make all targets within Very Close range lose a Hope. If a target is not able to lose a Hope, they must instead mark 2 Stress.

name: Demanding Crowd
description: A group of tourists from across the region, here to make wishes and be seen participating in the local custom. Demanding, incurious, and unwittingly making everything worse.

features.name: Horde (2d4)
features.description: When the Demanding Crowd has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 2d4 physical damage instead.

features.name: Antisocial Entitlement
features.description: The members of this crowd believe that they're owed fulfillment of their wishes. Rolls to convince them to disperse or give up on making wishes in the glade are made with disadvantage.

features.name: Heckling & Harassment
features.description: Spend a Fear to force all targets within Far range to make a Presence Reaction Roll or mark 2 Stress. If the Demanding Crowd has half or more of their HP marked or all their Stress marked, targets that fail only mark 1 Stress.

features.name: Panicked Trampling
features.description: When Demanding Crowd marks HP or Stress, they move a Far distance around the scene, trampling over any target in their way. Anyone they move past/through must succeed on a Strength Reaction Roll or be knocked over and trampled, taking 2d6 physical damage.
features.trigger: marks HP or Stress

name: Demonic Hound Pack
description: Des chiens surnaturels éclairés de l’intérieur par le feu infernal.

features.name: Horde (2d4+1)
features.description: Quand la Meute a coché la moitié ou plus de ses PV, son attaque standard inflige 2d4+1 dégâts physiques à la place.

features.name: Dreadhowl
features.description: Faites perdre un Espoir à toutes les cibles à Portée Très Proche. Si une cible ne peut pas perdre d’Espoir, elle doit cocher 2 Stress à la place.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: Quand la Meute réussit une attaque contre un PJ, vous gagnez une Peur.
features.trigger: Quand la meute réussit une attaque contre un pj, vous gagnez une peur

name: Dollmaker Hag
description: A hunched-over faerie with large, chitinous clawed hands and a terrifying grin with rows of sharp teeth.

features.name: Disturbing Presence
features.description: PCs that roll with Fear within Close range of the Hag must mark a Stress.

features.name: Stitch You Right Up
features.description: When the Hag is within Melee range of an ally, mark a Stress and the ally clears a HP.

features.name: Not This Time
features.description: When the hag takes Major or greater damage, mark a Stress to reduce the damage by 1d8.
features.trigger: takes Major or greater damage

features.name: How Dreadful! (Phase Change)
features.description: When the Dollmaker marks their last HP, replace them with the Puppetmaster Hag and immediately spotlight them.
features.trigger: marks last HP

name: Domovoi
description: A household spirit bound to the hearth, the Domovoi rewards respect and punishes neglect.

features.name: Bound to the Home
features.description: The Domovoi cannot leave the house it is bound to. The Domovoi remembers everything that has happened in the house — words spoken, promises broken, secrets kept.

features.name: The Weight of Ash
features.description: Mark a Stress to let the Domovoi remind intruders of unspoken guilt or broken customs. A target within Close range must make an Instinct Reaction Roll. If they fail, they take 2d6+2 direct magical damage and lose a Hope. If they succeed they must mark a Stress.

features.name: Shifting the Room
features.description: Mark a Stress to rearrange the scene. Move a number of targets equal to the amount of Fear in the GM's pool up to Close range.

name: Doppelhund
description: A vicious magical six-legged hellhound with glowing red eyes and two long, toothy squid arms. They confuse enemies by conjuring an illusory duplicate of themself.

features.name: Blink Beast
features.description: When combat begins, place a duplicate within Close range of the Doppelhund. The duplicate can move when the Doppelhund is spotlighted, and the Doppelhund can target enemies with attacks as if they're in the same place as their duplicate. The Doppelhund occupies both places until attacked, at which point you roll a d6 to determine whether the attack is targeting the Doppelhund (even number) or their duplicate (odd number). Attacks against the duplicate automatically fail. A successful attack against the Doppelhund dispels the duplicate until you spend a Fear to conjure it again.

features.name: Double Lash
features.description: When the Doppelhund makes a standard attack, you can mark a Stress to make an additional standard attack. If both attacks target the same creature, combine the damage.
features.trigger: When the Doppelhund makes a standard attack

name: Dragon Knight
description: A knight clad in dragonplate armor and a helmet in the shape of a dragon's head.

features.name: High Ground
features.description: The Knight gains a +2 bonus to attack rolls when attacking a target from above.

features.name: Enhanced Critical
features.description: The Knight's attack roll counts as a critical success if the result on the d20 is a 19 or a 20.

features.name: Leaping Strike
features.description: Mark a Stress and choose a target within Far range. The Knight moves into Melee range of the target and makes a standard attack against them. On a success, gain a +1d12 bonus to the damage roll and the target must mark an Armor Slot without receiving its benefits (they can still use armor to reduce the damage).

name: Drakona Elementalist
description: A drakona who wields elemental power.

features.name: One with the Elements
features.description: The Drakona is resistant to damage from elemental sources.

features.name: Elemental Breath
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against all targets within Very Close range in front of the Drakona. Targets the Drakona succeeds against take 2d8 magic damage.

features.name: Elemental Explosion
features.description: Spend a Fear to make a point within Far range erupt with elemental energy. All targets within Very Close range of that point must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 1d20 magic damage. Targets who succeed take half damage.

features.name: Residual Elements
features.description: When the Drakona deals Major or greater damage, residual elements Burn the target until they're extinguished with a successful Finesse Roll. While Burning, the PC takes 2d6 magic damage when they make an action roll.
features.trigger: When the Drakona deals Major or greater damage

name: Drone
description: A small flying machine surveying the landscape.

features.name: Sonic Pulse
features.description: Mark a Stress to emit a high pitched sound out to all targets within Very Close range. Those targets must mark 1d4 Stress.

features.name: Floodlight
features.description: Spend a Fear to Illuminate a target within Far range until the Drone is defeated or the Illuminated target becomes Hidden. While the target is Illuminated their Evasion is halved.

features.name: Crash Landing
features.description: When the Drone marks their last HP, they fall from the sky and explode, dealing 2d12 physical damage to all targets within Very Close range. Hauler Haulers have mechanical crab-like legs and are built to transport goods, bearing the large weight upon their back.
features.trigger: When the Drone marks their last HP

name: Dullahan
description: A headless centaur in fae armor with a flaming spine whip who is trailed by the flying heads of those it has killed.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Dullahan can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Geis
features.description: Choose a target within Far range to make a Presence Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target is Cursed until the Dullahan is defeated. While Cursed, the target's Fear die is a d20. On a roll with Fear, the target loses a Hope.

features.name: Death's Head
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against three targets within Far range. If the target is Cursed, the Dullahan has advantage. On a success, the target takes 2d6+10 magic damage.

features.name: Nightshod Hooves
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against all targets behind the Dullahan within Very Close range. Targets the Dullahan succeeds against take 2d20 physical damage and are knocked back to Far range.

features.name: Stag Charge
features.description: Countdown (Loop 1d6). When the Dullahan is in the spotlight for the first time, activate the countdown. When it triggers, move the Dullahan in a straight line to a point within Far range. All targets within its path must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 2d8+4 magic damage. Targets who succeed take half damage.
features.trigger: first spotlight

name: Dwarf Cleric
description: A dwarf with divine powers.

features.name: Magnetize
features.description: Mark a Stress to make two metals the Dwarf can see move towards each other. An affected target can resist moving with a successful Strength Reaction Roll. Targets who collide take 2d6 physical damage.

features.name: Dwarven Courage
features.description: The Dwarf or an ally in Melee range drinks from a flask of alcohol and clears 1d4 Stress. A non-Dwarf ally must succeed on a Reaction Roll (12) or their Difficulty is reduced by 2 until the end of the scene.

features.name: Shield
features.description: Once per scene, when an attack from Close range or further is made against the Dwarf or an ally within Very Close range, you can mark a Stress to create an energy shield that covers an area of Very Close range. All creatures within the shield take no damage from the attack, then the shield disappears.

name: Dwarf Ironclad
description: A dwarf equipped head-to-toe in armor.

features.name: Armor Shedding
features.description: The Dwarf's Difficulty is permanently reduced by 1 for each successful attack made against them.

features.name: Immovable Object
features.description: An attack that would move the Dwarf moves them one fewer range (for example, Far becomes Close).

features.name: Box In
features.description: Mark a Stress to choose a target within Melee range to focus on. That target has disadvantage on attack rolls when they're within Melee range of the Dwarf. The Dwarf can only focus on one target at a time.

features.name: Attack of Opportunity
features.description: When a target leaves Melee range of the Dwarf, make a standard attack against them.
features.trigger: When a target leaves Melee range of the Dwarf

name: Electric Eels
description: Un essaim de prédateurs aquatiques électriques.

features.name: Horde (2d4+1)
features.description: Quand les Anguilles ont coché la moitié ou plus de leurs PV, leur attaque standard inflige 2d4+1 dégâts physiques à la place.

features.name: Paralyzing Shock
features.description: Faites une attaque standard contre toutes les cibles à Portée Très Proche. Vous gagnez une Peur pour chaque cible qui coche des PV.

name: Elephant
description: A large creature with tusks and a long prehensile trunk.

features.name: An Elephant Never Forgets
features.description: The Elephant has advantage on attack rolls made against targets they have already dealt damage to.

features.name: Trample
features.description: Spend a Fear and move in a line up to Close range. Make an attack against all targets in the Elephant's path. Targets the Elephant succeeds against take 2d8 + 2 physical damage. Flock of Ravens Giant Toad Ravens are considered ill omens, appearing at fateful times to observe, attack, or offer cryptic words. Giant Snake Giant Snakes lurk in trees and long grass. They focus on smaller humanoids as their prey, which they are large enough to swallow whole.

name: Elf Blade-Dancer
description: An elf dual-wielding swords.

features.name: Combo Move
features.description: Mark a Stress and make two standard attacks against a target in Melee range. If both are successful, combine the damage.

features.name: Parry and Riposte
features.description: When an attack against the Elf from Melee range misses, you can mark a Stress to make a standard attack against the attacker.
features.trigger: When an attack against the Elf from Melee range misses

name: Elite Hive Collector
description: Seekers for Mother, they collect what might please her.

features.name: Sleeping Sting
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, they take2d6+8physical damage and must make a Instinct Reaction Roll (15). On a failure, they become Asleep until they take damage, or until1d4hours have passed. WhileAsleep, a PC cannot be spotlighted.

features.name: Tangle Net
features.description: Mark a Stressto make a target within Close range roll an Agility Reaction Roll (13). On a failure, they are Restrainedand Vulnerableuntil they succeed on a Strength or Agility Action Roll (15).

name: Elite Soldier
description: Un écuyer en armure ou un roturier expérimenté cherchant à progresser.

features.name: Reinforce
features.description: Déplacez-vous à Portée de Mêlée d’un allié et faites une attaque standard contre une cible à Portée Très Proche. En cas de réussite, infligez 2d10+2 dégâts physiques et l’allié peut retirer un Stress.

features.name: Vassal's Loyalty
features.description: Quand le Soldat est à Portée Très Proche d’un chevalier ou autre noble qui subirait des dégâts, déplacez-vous à Portée de Mêlée de celui-ci et subissez les dégâts à sa place.

name: Exiled Scoundrel
description: A criminal expelled from their home to perish

features.name: Soul Scarred
features.description: The Scoundrel can mark a stress to use its experience.

features.name: Garrote
features.description: Make an attack against a target. If the Scoundrel is Hidden when making the attack it automatically succeeds. On a success, mark a Stress to deal 2d10 physical damage and is Restrained. While Restrained, the target must mark a Stress when they make an action roll. The target can break free, clearing the condition with a successful Strength Roll or is freed automatically if the Scoundrel takes Major or greater damage.

features.name: Shadow Step
features.description: Mark a Stress and choose a point within Far range that is in shadow. The Scoundrel becomes Hidden and moves to that point.

name: Faerie Trickster
description: A faerie that likes to play pranks.

features.name: Invisible Hand
features.description: Summon an invisible spectral hand within Far range. The Faerie can manipulate an item with the hand and move it up to Close range.

features.name: Antimagic Field
features.description: Mark a Stress and choose a point within Close range. An antimagic field appears up to Very Close range of that point until the Faerie is defeated. No magic can pass within this area and magical effects are dispelled.

features.name: Cut Shadow
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, spend a Fear to separate their shadow from their body. Summon an Animated Shadow in Melee range of the target who immediately takes the spotlight. The target's shadow reappears when the Animated Shadow marks their last HP. Fauns Fauns are humanoids with goat-like features such as their caprine legs and horns. Curious by nature, many Fauns are nomadic, traveling far and wide. As they travel, their body adapts to be more humanlike or goatlike depending on the environment.

name: Failed Experiment
description: Une expérience nécromantique magique qui a mal tourné, le laissant déformé et maladroit.

features.name: Warped Fortitude
features.description: L’Expérience est résistante aux dégâts physiques.

features.name: Overwhelm
features.description: Quand une cible attaquée par l’Expérience a d’autres adversaires à Portée Très Proche, l’Expérience inflige le double de dégâts.

features.name: Lurching Lunge
features.description: Placez l’Expérience sous le projecteur comme action MJ supplémentaire au lieu de dépenser une Peur.

name: Fallen Soldier
description: A soldier of the Old World with a chainmail coif but no head.

features.name: Remembrance
features.description: Mark a Stress and choose a target within Melee range to make a Presence Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target takes 2d6 direct magic damage and is made Vulnerable until they next act as they see visions of the Soldier’s last moments.

features.name: Death Save
features.description: Once per scene when the Soldier marks its last HP, if it wasn’t from a critical success, roll a d20. On a 10 or higher, it gains 1 HP and immediately takes the spotlight.

name: Faun Janus
description: A large two headed faun, wielding weapons in both hands.

features.name: Indecision
features.description: When you spotlight the Faun, roll a d4. On a 1, the Faun's heads can't agree, ending their turn.

features.name: Two Heads, One Mind
features.description: Mark a Stress to make two standard attacks when the Faun acts. If they are against the same target, combine the damage.

features.name: Battering Ram
features.description: Spend a Fear to have the Faun charge at a structure within Close range they could feasibly topple (such as a statue or a load-bearing wall) causing it to collapse. Targets in range of the toppled structure must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take 2d10 physical damage.

name: Fear Gorta, Hollow-Mouth
description: A famine saint and curse in the same coat. Crows perch on his shoulders to hear who is spared.

features.name: Empty Pantry
features.description: Choose a point within Close. Until cleared, that area becomes Withered: rations spoil, conjured food fails, and creatures who start there must mark a Stress unless they spend a Hope.

features.name: Alms or Ashes
features.description: The first time a PC refuses meaningful aid to a helpless NPC in the scene, increase this adversary's Difficulty by +2 until end of scene.

features.name: Eyes Like Winter
features.description: When a PC refuses aid or hoards supplies, mark a Stress to give that PC disadvantage on their next Presence or Instinct roll.
features.trigger: A PC refuses aid or hoards supplies

name: Fellborn Berserker
description: A soul who has lost much of their mind to the shadows

features.name: Soul Scarred
features.description: The Berserker can mark a stress to use its experience.

features.name: Raging Fury
features.description: When the Berserker has marked 4 or greater HP, they become Enraged. All damage the Berserker does while Enraged is direct damage.

features.name: Death Spin
features.description: Spend a Fear to make a standard attack against all targets within Very Close range.

features.name: Vicious Blow
features.description: On a successful attack, mark a Stress to cause all PCs in Far range to lose a Hope. Tier 2 - Marsh Monsters During the Exodus, the seas rose and flooded the valley. The shadows left by the gods’ retreat have changed the people and creatures that once called this place home, turning them into deadly monsters. The waters have created more than a dozen reeking swamps ripe with danger.

name: Festering Ghast
description: A pale, long limbed, eater of flesh, dropping in blood and gore

features.name: Feast
features.description: If the ghast has any marked HP, spend a Fear to eat from a corpse and clear a HP

features.name: Horrifying
features.description: Targets who mark HP from the Ghasts attacks must also mark a Stress.

features.name: Out of Nowhere
features.description: The Ghast has advantage on attacks if they are Hidden

features.name: Seize Your Moment
features.description: SpendFearto spotlightdGhoul allies. Attacks they make while spotlighted in this way deal half damage.

features.name: Revolting stench
features.description: When a PC fails with fear within Close range of the Banshee, they must mark a stress.  Chapter Two: Adversaries Tier Two

name: Firbolg Cowboy
description: A firbo!g wearing tattered leathers and a cowboy hat.

features.name: Marksman
features.description: Mark a Stress to gain advantage on an attack roll if the Firbolg hasn't moved this GM turn.

features.name: Lasso
features.description: If the Firbolg is mounted, make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success the target is bound by rope held by the Firbolg. The target moves with the Firbolg and is Restrained and Vulnerable until they break free with a successful Finesse Roll.

features.name: Crackshot
features.description: Mark a Stress to make a standard attack against a target within Far range. On a success, deal 2d8+3 physical damage. Firbolg Cowboy Cowboys live on the frontiers and arm themselves with black powder weapons. Firbolg Farmer Farmers are natural herbalists that can heal almost any affliction, for a price. Minotaur Bully Bullies are aggressive bull-headed Firbolgs that pick on those they perceive to be weak.

name: Firbolg Farmer
description: A firbolg farmer who grows rare plants.

features.name: Herbal Remedies
features.description: The Firbolg can create consumables from herbal ingredients that can clear HP, Stress, or conditions.

features.name: Deadly Companion
features.description: Once per scene, summon a Bear, Dire Wolf, or similar Tier 1 animal adversary under the Firbolg's control. The adversary appears at Close range and is immediately spotlighted.

features.name: Get Off My Land
features.description: When the Firbolg first encounters the PCs, they must succeed on a group Presence Roll or each mark a Stress.
features.trigger: When the Firbolg first encounters the PCs

name: Flock of Ravens
