description: A conspiracy of large ravens, spinning in a whirl of black feathers.

features.name: Bad Omen
features.description: Rolls with Fear against the Ravens generate 2 Fear.

features.name: Cacophonous Caws
features.description: All targets within Very Close range must make a Presence Reaction Roll. Targets who fail lose a Hope. Giant Toads live in jungles and can coexist with Ribbets. The Toad will taste anything to see if it is food.

name: Fungril Deathcap
description: A monstrously large fungril with multiple mushroom caps growing upon it.

features.name: Ramp Up
features.description: You must spend a Fear to spotlight the Fungril. While spotlighted, they can make their standard attack against all targets within range.

features.name: Overwhelming Force
features.description: Targets who mark HP from the Fungril's standard attack are knocked back to Very Close range.

features.name: Confusion Spores
features.description: Mark a Stress to release disorienting spores affecting all non-Fungril targets within Very Close range. Targets must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or become Confused until they succeed on an Instinct Roll. While Confused, targets must roll a d4 when they act. On a 1, they attack the nearest ally instead of their intended action.

name: Fungril Horde
description: A group offungrils working together for a common cause.

features.name: Mushroom Circle
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, deal 2d6+l magic damage and the Horde encircles the target. The target is Vulnerable while the Horde encircles them and they must mark a Stress when they act.

name: Fungril Mort
description: A tall, thin fungril with a stench of death and decay.

features.name: Raise Dead
features.description: Mark a Stress to create a Tier 1 or Tier 2 Zombie from a corpse.

features.name: l/l/e Are One
features.description: Once per scene, spend a Fear to spotlight all other adversaries within Far range. Attacks they make while spotlighted in this way deal half damage.

features.name: Decay
features.description: When the target of the Fungril's attack marks HP, you can mark a Stress to reduce the target's highest trait by 1d4 until they succeed on a roll with that trait or their next rest. G Galapa Galapa are turtle-like humanoids. In Galapa communities, able-bodied Galapa leave to immerse themselves in the world, returning once a year in a large celebratory gathering to share their experiences and knowledge. Galapa Chronomancer Chronomancers are mages who control the flow of time experienced by others. Galapa Ninja Ninjas are spies and scouts who cloak themselves in black and move with speed and stealth little associated with Galapa. Galapa Shell Warrior Shell Warriors uses their hard shell for offense rather than defense.
features.trigger: When the target of the Fungril's attack marks HP

name: Galapa Ninja
description: A galapa in dark clothing trained in martial arts and infiltration.

features.name: Shadow Step
features.description: If the Galapa is in shadow, you can mark a Stress to teleport them to another shadow within Far range, becoming Hidden. You can mark an additional Stress to teleport a target Restrained by the Galapa.

features.name: Garotte
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Melee range using a piece of wire. On a success, the target takes 2d6+2 direct physical damage, and the wire is wrapped around their neck. The target is Restrained and Vulnerable and can't speak or cast spells until they break free, ending all conditions, with a successful Strength or Finesse Roll.

features.name: Deflect Projectile
features.description: When a ranged attack made against the Galapa misses, you can mark a Stress to deflect the projectile onto a target within Very Close range, dealing half damage.
features.trigger: When a ranged attack made against the Galapa misses

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Galapa makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When the Galapa makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Galapa Shell Warrior
description: A galapa warrior with a scarred shell.

features.name: Shell Bounce
features.description: Make an attack against all targets within Melee range. Targets the Galapa succeeds against take 2d6+2 physical damage.

features.name: Cannonball
features.description: Mark a Stress to tuck into the Galapa's shell and move to Melee range of a target within Close range. Make an attack against the target. On a success, deal 2dlO+5 physical damage.

features.name: Retract
features.description: When the Galapa takes physical damage, you can mark a Stress to take half damage. Gargoyle Gargoyles are winged creatures made from stone that like to perch on rooftops. Some like to cause trouble while others are paid in food to spy or to watch over homes for protection.
features.trigger: When the Galapa takes physical damage

name: Gargoyle
description: A stone sentinel that perches upon rooftops.

features.name: Stoneskin
features.description: The Gargoyle is resistant to physical damage.

features.name: Kamikaze
features.description: When the Gargoyle moves to Close range and makes a successful standard attack, you can mark a HP to deal 2d12+4 physical damage instead of their standard damage.

features.name: Rock
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Close range, dealing 2d6 physical damage on a success. Us Faerie often have to watch out for hungry Gargoyles in urban spaces. -	Eryn Genie Genies are beings with gaseous bodies and powerful magical abilities. They live within grandly designed extradimensional spaces they have constructed inside otherwise mundane items to avoid scrutiny. A Genie’s home is magically held together by the Genie’s own essence. Consequently, if the item the Genie’s home is created within is claimed by someone, the Genie becomes bound to them and must act on their behalf. A Genie can become free again by granting wishes. After three wishes, the Genie’s essence is used up, their home is destroyed, and they are free to construct a new home elsewhere. Some Genies might freely grant wishes for other purposes, such as to adventurers who free it from servitude.

name: Giant Beastmaster
description: Un guerrier vêtu de cuir portant un fouet et un arc massif.

features.name: Two as One
features.description: Quand le Maître des Bêtes est mis sous le projecteur, vous pouvez aussi placer sous le projecteur un adversaire animal de Tier 1 actuellement sous son contrôle.

features.name: Pinning Strike
features.description: Faites une attaque standard contre une cible. En cas de réussite, clouez-la à une surface proche. La cible clouée est Entravée jusqu’à ce qu’elle se libère avec un jet de Finesse ou de Force réussi.

features.name: Deadly Companion
features.description: Deux fois par scène, invoquez un Ours, un Loup Sinistre ou un adversaire animal de Tier 1 similaire sous le contrôle du Maître des Bêtes. L’adversaire apparaît à Portée Proche et est immédiatement mis sous le projecteur.

name: Giant Brawler
description: Un géant particulièrement musclé brandissant un marteau de guerre plus grand qu’un humain.

features.name: Battering Ram
features.description: Chargez un objet inanimé à Portée Proche et détruisez-le. Toutes les cibles à Portée Très Proche de l’objet doivent réussir un jet de Réaction d’Agilité ou subir 2d4+3 dégâts physiques des éclats.

features.name: Bloody Reprisal
features.description: Quand le Bagarreur coche 2 PV ou plus suite à une attaque à Portée Très Proche, faites une attaque standard contre l’attaquant. En cas de réussite, infligez 2d6+15 dégâts physiques au lieu des dégâts standard.
features.trigger: Quand le bagarreur coche 2 pv ou plus suite à une attaque à portée très proche

features.name: Momentum
features.description: Quand le Bagarreur réussit une attaque contre un PJ, vous gagnez une Peur.
features.trigger: Quand le bagarreur réussit une attaque contre un pj, vous gagnez une peur

name: Giant Eagle
description: Un oiseau de proie géant aux serres tachées de sang.

features.name: Flight
features.description: En vol, l’Aigle gagne un bonus de +3 à sa Difficulté.

features.name: Deadly Dive
features.description: Attaquez une cible à Portée Loin. En cas de réussite, infligez 2d10+2 dégâts physiques et renversez la cible, la rendant Vulnérable jusqu’à sa prochaine action.

features.name: Take Off
features.description: Faites une attaque contre une cible à Portée Très Proche. En cas de réussite, infligez 2d4+3 dégâts physiques et la cible doit réussir un jet de Réaction d’Agilité ou être temporairement Entravée dans les serres massives de l’Aigle.

name: Giant Fungus
description: A huge mushroom with an umbrella cap.

features.name: Plant
features.description: The Fungus can't move.

features.name: Toxic Spores
features.description: Mark a Stress to release toxic spores. All targets within Close range must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or become Poisoned. While Poisoned, they must mark a Stress to make action rolls until they clear HP or they succeed on a Knowledge Roll (15).

name: Giant Landbreaker
description: A towering geomancer adorned in gems and rocks, manipulating the earth itself.

features.name: Speak to the Land
features.description: When paying the cost for environment Fear features, instead of spending Fear, the Landbreaker canmark the same number of Stress.

features.name: Choke-Point
features.description: Spend a Fearto pick a point with Far range. Landbreaker pulls up the earth and create two mostly-parallel walls a Close range high and long. They leave only a narrow path only a Melee range wide.

features.name: Earthen Shield
features.description: When Landbreaker would take damage from beyond Very Close range, mark a Stressto halve the incoming damage.
features.trigger: When Landbreaker would take damage from beyond Very Close range

name: Giant Recruit
description: Un combattant géant portant une armure empruntée.

features.name: Minion (7)
features.description: La Recrue est vaincue quand elle subit n’importe quels dégâts. Pour chaque tranche de 7 dégâts qu’un PJ inflige à la Recrue, vainquez un Minion supplémentaire à portée que l’attaque toucherait.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Choisissez une cible et placez sous le projecteur toutes les Recrues Géantes à Portée Proche d’elle. Ces Minions se déplacent à Portée de Mêlée de la cible et font un jet d’attaque partagé. En cas de réussite, ils infligent 5 dégâts physiques chacun. Combinez ces dégâts.

name: Giant Spider
description: An arachnid the size of a small human with markings that help it blend into its environment

features.name: Wall Crawler
features.description: The Giant Spider can climb and walk on any surface, including its own webs without being slowed. Additionally, the Spider’s standard attack does 2d8+10 physical damage to Restrained targets.

features.name: Web Maker
features.description: Mark a Stress and choose a point within Far range. A Close area around that point becomes covered in Webs. All targets in that area must make an Agility Reaction Roll (14). Targets that fail are Restrained. Targets that succeed can only move within a Very Close range as their regular movement while in the area.

features.name: Patient Hunter
features.description: If a target enters an area of webs created by the Spider, it can move into Melee range and make a standard attack at advantage..
features.trigger: If a target enters an area of webs created by the Spider

name: Gladestalker
description: A large simiah with four arms dressed in rough leathers and hunched forward

features.name: Trampler
features.description: Mark a Stress to move to a point within Far range in a straight line. Any targets in the Gladestalker’s path must make a Strength Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target takes 2d8 physical damage and is knocked back to a Close range of where they were.

features.name: Thousand Hand Slap
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, mark a Stress to deal 4d6+3 physical damage. For any 6s rolled, roll another d6. Combine this damage.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Gladestalker makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When the Gladestalker makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Glomtower Thrall
description: A towering being of sorrow and service, forever doomed to

features.name: Slow
features.description: When you spotlight the Thrall and they don’t have a token on their stat block, they can’t act yet. Place a token on their stat block and describe what they’re preparing to do. When you spotlight the Thrall and they have a token on their stat block, clear the token and they can act.

features.name: Dense But Clumsy
features.description: An attack that would move the Thrall moves them two fewer range steps, and the Thrall must succeed on a Reaction Roll (10). On a failure, it falls over and becomes temporarily RestrainedandVulnerable. When it is no longerRestrained, it also is no longerVulnerable.

features.name: Cage The Judged
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, the target takes2d12+7 physical damage, is Restrained, and has disadvantage on attack rolls until they succeed on a Strength or Agility Action Roll (16).

features.name: Cage Slam
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, the target and any PCs in the cage take2d12+15physical damage.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Thrall makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.

features.name: Kick
features.description: Mark a Stresswhen the Thrall marks 2 or more HP from a PC attack within Very Close range. Then, immediately make an attack against the attacker. On a success, they take2d6+7physical damage and are pushed to Far range.

name: Gnome Gunslinger
description: A gnome armed with a large firearm.

features.name: Hip Fire
features.description: The Gnome has disadvantage on attack rolls if a PC is within Very Close range.

features.name: Focused Strike
features.description: Mark a Stress and make a standard attack against a target, aiming for a specific weak point. On a success, deal 2d8+6 physical damage instead of their standard damage.

features.name: Maiming Strike
features.description: Mark a Stress and make a standard attack against a target, aiming for their leg. On a success, deal 2d8 physical damage and the target can only move up to Very Close range until they clear HP.

name: Gnome Tinkerer
description: A gnome who builds contraptions.

features.name: Jetpack
features.description: Mark a Stress to move to Far range instead of Close range before taking an action.

features.name: Quick Fix
features.description: Mark a Stress to clear a HP or Stress from a mechanical construction.

features.name: Flashbang Grenade
features.description: Throw a grenade to a point within Close range. All targets within Very Close range of that point must make an Instinct Reaction Roll. Targets who fail become Vulnerable until they next act and have disadvantage on their next action roll.

features.name: Arc Lightning
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against a target within Close range. On a success, deal 2d10 magic damage and half damage to any additional targets within Close range of the initial target that the attack would succeed against. Goblinoids Goblinoids are fae humanoid creatures who are inclined to chaotic behavior, often acting on impulse. They tend to form tribes in the wilds, often raiding settlements to obtain loot. Goblin The standard goblinoid is the Goblin, a small creature with large eyes and rounded ears. Goblins are nimble and mouthy, constantly taunting their opponents in fights. Hobblin Hobblins (also known as Bugbears) are tall, brutish goblinoids with brown fur on their bodies. Hobgoblin Hobgoblins are a mix of Hobblin and Goblin. They are taller than most other goblinoids and get intensely fixated on their tasks, making them martially proficient and capable leaders of other goblinoids. Yoblin Yoblins have more angular features, sharper teeth, and are more aggressive than Goblins. They constantly stir up trouble and find themselves in the thick of the action, with no sense of self-preservation.

name: Gobstalker
description: A fleshy, floating ball of teeth with ten jointed antennae rising from its crown, each bearing a different-colored eye.

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: The Gobstalker can be spotlighted up to three times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Anti-mystica-halitosis
features.description: A Gobstalker's breath is so rancid it creates an invisible miasma that negates magical effects. Any attack that deals magic damage or creates a magical effect in front of the Gobstalker within Close range automatically fails. This feature does not affect the Gobstalker's own features.

features.name: Eyestalks
features.description: Mark a Stress to attack a target within Far range. On a hit, spend a Fear to choose an effect or roll a d10 for a random magical effect from the corresponding beam.

name: Golden Ooze
description: A moving mound of golden slime with a treasure visible at the center.

features.name: Precious Loot
features.description: The Ooze has approximately 1 bag of gold (or other items worth the same value) within them. A PC can mark a HP and make a Finesse Roll (10) to pull 1d4 handfuls of gold from the Ooze.

features.name: Sense Gold
features.description: Mark a Stress to make a standard attack with advantage against a target carrying gold within range. On a success, deal damage and the target loses a handful of gold.

features.name: Consume Item
features.description: Countdown (4). When the Ooze is in the spotlight for the first time, activate the countdown. It ticks down when a PC rolls with Fear against the Ooze. When it triggers, the last of the gold is consumed.
features.trigger: Countdown (4)

name: Gorgon
description: Un humanoïde écailleux à tête de serpents avec un arc doré, enragé que sa paix ait été troublée.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: La Gorgone peut être mise sous le projecteur jusqu’à deux fois par tour du MJ. Dépensez une Peur comme d’habitude pour la placer sous le projecteur.

features.name: Sunsear Arrows
features.description: Quand la Gorgone réussit une attaque standard, la cible Brille jusqu’à la fin de la scène et ne peut pas devenir Cachée. Les jets d’attaque contre une cible Brillante ont l’avantage.

features.name: Crown of Serpents
features.description: Faites un jet d’attaque contre une cible à Portée de Mêlée en utilisant les serpents protecteurs de la Gorgone. En cas de réussite, infligez 2d10+4 dégâts physiques et la cible doit cocher un Stress.

features.name: Petrifying Gaze
features.description: Quand la Gorgone subit des dégâts d’une attaque à Portée Proche, forcez l’attaquant à faire un jet de Réaction d’Instinct. En cas d’échec, il commence à se transformer en pierre, cochant un PV et démarrant un Compte à rebours de Pétrification (4). Ce compte à rebours décompte quand la Gorgone est attaquée. Quand il se déclenche, la cible doit faire un mouvement de mort. Si la Gorgone est vaincue, tous les comptes à rebours de pétrification prennent fin.
features.trigger: Quand la gorgone subit des dégâts d’une attaque à portée proche

features.name: Momentum
features.description: Quand la Gorgone réussit une attaque contre un PJ, vous gagnez une Peur.
features.trigger: Quand la gorgone réussit une attaque contre un pj, vous gagnez une peur

name: Gorilla
description: A large ape.

features.name: Beastly Power
features.description: When you initially roll damage, if you roll the maximum value on a die, also roll a new die of that type and add it to the total.

features.name: Ragdoll Slam
features.description: Mark a Stress to attack a target within Very Close range. On a success, deal 2d12+4 physical damage.

name: Great Worm
description: A large worm with diamond teeth that carves through solid rock.

features.name: Burrower
features.description: The Worm can move unhindered through solid rock, leaving a tunnel in their wake.

features.name: Earth Eruption
features.description: Mark a Stress to have the Worm burst out of the ground. All creatures within Very Close range must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or be knocked over, becoming Vulnerable until they next act.

features.name: Tail Swipe
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Close range. On a success, deal 2d10+5 physical damage and the target is knocked back to Close range.

features.name: Swallow
features.description: When the Worm deals Major or greater damage on a standard attack, you can spend a Fear to swallow the target. While swallowed, the target is Restrained within the Worm and must mark a Stress when they act. If the Worm takes Major or greater damage, all swallowed creatures are released, taking ldlO+3 physical damage and moving to Very Close range of the Worm.
features.trigger: When the Worm deals Major or greater damage on a standard attack

features.name: Tail Swipe
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Close range. On a success, deal 4d10+10 physical damage and the target is knocked back to Far range.

features.name: Swallow
features.description: When the Worm deals Major or greater damage on a standard attack, you can spend a Fear to swallow the target. While swallowed, the creature is Restrained within the Worm and must mark a Stress when they act. If the Worm takes Major or greater damage, all swallowed creatures are released, taking 1d20 + 5 physical damage and moving to Very Close range of the Worm.
features.trigger: When the Worm deals Major or greater damage on a standard attack

features.name: Split
features.description: When the Worm has 4 or more HP marked, you can spend a Fear to split them into two Great Worms (with no marked HP or Stress). Immediately spotlight them both. Wraiths Wraiths are sentient forces of entropy that form in the space between realms. They break through where reality thins from anomalies and paradoxes occurring in the natural order of the universe. Wraiths act based on what will cause the greatest disorder in the long term. Adversaries who seek to bring wide-scale destruction might dare to summon Wraiths to enlist them in enacting their plans, risking madness if the Wraith turns on them. Death Wraith Death Wraiths appear when there are anomalous crossings between the Mortal Realm and the Valley of Death, such as mortals crossing the Veil or acts of necromancy. Time Wraith Time Wraiths are drawn towards temporal paradoxes, such as time loops or creatures displaced in time. Void Wraith Void Wraiths are drawn to anomalies in space such as rifts between worlds.
features.trigger: When the Worm has 4 or more HP marked

name: Gremlin
description: A small humanoid with large claws and a greasy body.

features.name: Grease Trail
features.description: The Gremlin can choose to leave a trail of grease behind them when they move. A creature moving through the grease must make an Agility Reaction Roll (10). On a failure, the creature falls down, becoming Vulnerable until they next act, and ending their turn.

features.name: Grease
features.description: Lob grease at a point within Close range. The area within Very Close range of that point becomes slippery, acting like the Gremlin's Grease Trail passive.

features.name: Break Item
features.description: Mark a Stress to break an item within Melee range. If the item is PC armor, they must mark 2 Armor Slots without receiving its benefits. Broken items can be repaired as a downtime move.

features.name: Spark
features.description: Spend a Fear to ignite a continuous patch of grease, burning it away. All targets within Melee range of the grease must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take 2d8 magic damage and become Ignited until they're extinguished with a successful Finesse Roll. Vulnerable targets have disadvantage on this roll. While Ignited a target must mark a HP when they make an action roll. H Hags Hags are elderly witches who delight in causing the misery of others. Desperate people seek out Hags to make use of their power, but it always comes at a cost, with something precious taken in return for their services. When a group of Hags come together they form a coven. Being part of a coven strengthens the abilities of each Hag. Fatespinner Hag Fatespinner Hags have the ability to manipulate fates. They can perceive the nearterm future of individuals and affect minor but consequential details to change their fate.

name: Griffon
description: A creature with a lion's body and the head and wings of an eagle, often used as a winged mount.

features.name: From Above
features.description: When the Griffon succeeds on a standard attack from above a target, they deal 2d10+4 physical damage instead of their standard damage.

features.name: Aerial Drop
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Close range. On a success, mark a Stress to lift the target into the air and drop them within Close range. The target takes 1d20+5 direct physical damage and is knocked down, becoming Vulnerable until they next act. Manticore Rhino Beetle Manticores are ferocious beasts with a lion head and body, the wings of a dragon, and a spiked tail. A Rhino Beetle is a single-horned beetle the size of a rhino. Their thick shell combined with their ability to fly and burrow makes them a versatile and tough foe.

name: Grimcroak Mauler
description: A huge ribbet with thick arms that reach to the ground, dressed in skulls and leathers

features.name: Heavy Leap
features.description: Mark a Stress to leap anywhere within Far range and make an attack. On a success, the target takes 2d12+5 physical damage.

features.name: Grim Harvest
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against all targets in Very Close range. Targets the Mauler succeeds against take 2d12+5 physical damage. If a target marks 2 or more HP from this attack, they also lose a Hope. The Mauler then clears 1 HP.

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When the Mauler makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When the Mauler makes a successful attack against a PC

name: Hakturi
description: A doe-eyed creature of the wood in desperate need of your help.

features.name: Leech
features.description: When a PC encounters a Hakturi for the first time, all PCs within Close range gain a Hope. If PCs complete a long rest within Close range of a Hakturi, they must mark 1d4 Stress and their Hope die becomes a d10 until the Hakturi is defeated or the PCs rest again outside of its presence. For each Stress the PCs mark while in Close range of the Hakturi, the Hakturi gains a friend token.

features.name: Romp
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, deal 2d4+2 physical damage and the target must succeed on a Presence Reaction Roll or become temporarily Vulnerable. If the target fails, you can spend a friend token to immediately spotlight another Hakturi within Close range.

features.name: Empathy
features.description: When a befriended Hakturi takes damage from a once friendly PC, spend a Fear and all targets within Close range must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target must mark 2 Stress and their next attack is made at disadvantage.
features.trigger: takes damage from friendly PC

features.name: Evolve (Phase Change)
features.description: When the Hakturi accumulates 12 friend tokens, replace them with the Hakturi Mother and immediately spotlight them.
features.trigger: accumulates 12 friend tokens

name: Hakturi Mother
description: A beachball-sized fluffy forest creature that uses its adorable nature to subdue prey.

features.name: Hypnotic Pollen
features.description: Spend a Fear to force all targets within Very Close range to succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll (15). Targets that fail are Infatuated and see all Hakturi as allies. A target that is Infatuated must mark a Stress if any Hakturi marks HP within Far range of them. This effect lasts until the target succeeds on a Presence or Knowledge Roll. PCs who have been Infatuated during the scene have disadvantage on action rolls that target the Hakturi Mother.

features.name: Overwhelming Odds
features.description: Mark a Stress to spotlight a number of Hakturi equal to the Hakturi Mother's unmarked HP.

features.name: Pack of Lies
features.description: Twice per scene, spend a Fear to summon 2 Hakturi that appear within Close range.

features.name: Demure Disposition
features.description: When a target that has disadvantage fails a roll against the Hakturi Mother, they mark a Stress and you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: disadvantaged target fails roll

name: Halfling Sneak
description: A stealthy halfling rogue.

features.name: Lucky
features.description: The Halfling can reroll a 1 on an action roll.

features.name: Partners In Crime
features.description: If the Halfling makes a successful standard attack and another Halfling Sneak is within Melee range of the target, deal 2d6+6 physical damage instead of their standard damage.

features.name: Cloaked
features.description: Become Hidden until after the Halfling's next attack. Attacks made while Hidden from this feature have advantage.

name: Hauler
description: A crab-like machine bearing a large weight upon its back.

features.name: Slow
features.description: When you spotlight the Hauler and they don't have a token on their stat block, they can't act yet. Place a token on their stat block and describe what they're preparing to do. When you spotlight the Hauler and they have a token on their stat block, clear the token and they can act.

features.name: Release Cargo
features.description: Once per scene, mark a Stress to drop the Hauler's cargo onto all targets within Very Close range in front of the Hauler. All targets must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take 3d12 physical damage, becoming Restrained beneath the cargo until they free themselves with a Strength Roll. The Hauler loses their "Slow" passive feature.

features.name: Pin
features.description: When the Hauler makes a successful standard attack, you can mark a Stress to temporarily Restrain the target and force them to mark 2 Stress. Scrapper Scrappers pick apart metallic waste, keeping the valuable parts and incinerating the rest, which they eject as molten slag.
features.trigger: When the Hauler makes a successful standard attack

name: Hollow Captain
description: A suit of heavy armor that remembers its duty, even in death

features.name: Battle Tactics
features.description: If an ally is within Very Close range of the Captain, their difficulty is increased by 1.

features.name: Rally
features.description: Spend a Fear to clear a Stress from all allies in Close range.

features.name: Close Ranks
features.description: All allies within Far range move to a point within Close range. This point must move them closer to the Captain.

features.name: Charge
features.description: Countdown (Loop 1d4). When the Captain is in the spotlight for the first time, activate the countdown. When it triggers, all allies within Close range take the spotlight. Attacks they make while spotlighted in this way deal half damage. Tier 2 - Deathsworn Some of those who lurk in the shadows rail against the light and have nothing but hate in their hearts. These once enduring people have given up hope, and with it, pieces of their souls. These highwaymen consumed by the darkness now lurk along the edges of towns and roads
features.trigger: Countdown (Loop 1d4)

features.name: Inspiration Minis
features.description: (NSFW)Artisan Guild Rodburg Cultists of Melmor and Rescale Minatures' Unchained Raider Pack

name: Hulking Bodyguard
description: A giant, muscled creature who is paid handsomely to protect their client.

features.name: You Da Boss
features.description: When the Bodyguard enters the scene, designate one adversary to be their boss.

features.name: Outta My Way!
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against all targets within Close range. Targets the Bodyguard succeeds against take 2d12+6 physical damage and are knocked back to Far range.

features.name: I Got You, Boss!
features.description: When the Bodyguard's boss marks HP from an attacker in Close range of the Bodyguard, mark a Stress to move into Very Close range of the attacker and make a standard attack against them.
features.trigger: boss marks HP from attacker in Close range

features.name: Intimidating Presence
features.description: When a PC attacks the Bodyguard and misses, they lose a Hope.
features.trigger: PC misses attack

name: Hulking Fungril
description: An infestation of rot given humanoid form

features.name: Shifting Vines
features.description: The Fungril cannot be Restrained or made Vulnerable.

features.name: Enveloping Slam
features.description: Make a standard attack. On a success, mark a Stress to Restrain the target. While Restrained, the target moves with the Fungril. A target can end this effect with a successful Strength Roll. If the target fails the strength roll, they take 2d6 physical damage.

features.name: Living Shield
features.description: When the Fungril takes damage from an attack and has a creature Restrained, mark a Stress to take half the damage and the Restrained PC takes the other half.
features.trigger: When the Fungril takes damage from an attack and has a creature Restrained

name: Human Mob
description: A band of angry civilians armed with torches and pitch forks.

features.name: Call to Action
features.description: When the Mob acts, mark a Stress to rally bystanders to the cause. The Mob gains a HP, even if it would exceed their HP maximum.

features.name: Overwhelm
features.description: When the Mob marks HP from an attack within Melee range, you can mark a Stress to make a standard attack against the attacker. Human Supervillain Supervillains have a tragic backstory, suffering a misfortune that they then use to justify their evil plans.
features.trigger: When the Mob marks HP from an attack within Melee range

name: Ice Golem
description: An ice construct etched with runes.

features.name: Rune of Magic Protection
features.description: While the Golem has marked 3 or fewer HP, they have immunity to magic damage.

features.name: Ice Floor
features.description: Mark a Stress to make the floor up to Far range slick with ice. Targets must make a successful Agility Roll (12) to make any movement on the ice beyond Very Close range.

features.name: Pinball
features.description: If the Golem has used their Ice Floor action, mark a Stress to glide up to Close range across the ice and bounce off up to three surfaces or targets. Make a standard attack against all targets the Golem collides with.

features.name: Freeze
features.description: Spend a Fear to release a blast of cold energy up to Very Close range. All targets in range must make an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail are frozen in a block of ice. While frozen they are Restrained, can't be targeted, and can't act except to make a Strength Roll (13) to free themselves. Steel Golems are expensive, precisely built constructs trained and fine-tuned for combat.

name: Ice Spider
description: A large spider with razor sharp legs allowing it to grip icy surfaces.

features.name: Spicier Climb
features.description: The Spider can climb across walls or the ceiling.

features.name: Ice Web
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack to fling webbing at a target within Close range. On a success, the webbing flash-freezes on the target. The target is Restrained and must succeed on a Strength Roll (12) to break free.

features.name: Icy Death
features.description: Spend a Fear to dislodge a chunk of ice or icicles. All targets within Very Close range must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take 2d10 physical damage. Restrained targets automatically fail this roll.

name: Inferni Hellraiser
description: An inferni outlaw embracing their inner demonic inclinations to create chaos.

features.name: Daredevil
features.description: When the Inferni makes a standard attack against a target within Melee range, you can mark a Stress to have disadvantage on the attack roll but gain a +10 bonus to the damage roll.

features.name: Scattershot Bolts
features.description: Spend a Fear and make an attack against all targets in front of the Inferni within Very Close range. On a success, a target takes 2d6 magic damage.

features.name: Hellfire Burns
features.description: When the Inferni deals Major or greater damage, the target is Ignited until they're extinguished with a successful Finesse Roll. While Ignited, the PC must mark a HP when they make an action roll. Inferni Diabolist An Inferni Diabolist communes and strikes dark bargains with demons of the Circles Below. Inferni Hellraiser An Inferni Hellraiser is a rebel or outlaw cast out from society, always stirring up trouble from exile. Inferni Incubus/Succubus An Inferni Incubus or Succubus is a beautiful creature capable of magically entrancing others.
features.trigger: When the Inferni deals Major or greater damage

name: Inferni Incubus/Succubus
description: A beautiful inferni capable of magically seducing others.

features.name: Smitten
features.description: The Inferni has advantage on action rolls made against an Infatuated target.

features.name: Change Appearance
features.description: Mark a Stress to alter the Inferni's appearance. The Inferni can also change their sex and ancestry.

features.name: Infatuate
features.description: Spend a Fear to release pheromones to make a PC fall in love with the Inferni. The PC must succeed on a Presence Reaction Roll or become Infatuated. While Infatuated, they are in love with the Inferni and feel compelled to follow the Inferni's requests, but otherwise have free will. If another PC attacks the Inferni the Infatuated PC must mark a Stress. They can mark 2 Stress to make an Instinct Roll, ending the condition on a success and becoming immune to becoming Infatuated again.

features.name: Lover's Kiss
features.description: An Infatuated target within Melee range must make a Presence Reaction Roll. On a failure, they take 2d10 direct magic damage and you gain a Fear. Innards Innards are magical manifestations of parts of the body. Below is a map of the body in the form of a dungeon, consisting of connected rooms and paths. PCs might need to enter and traverse a body to change a destructive kaiju’s behavior, or cure a cursed ally, shrinking themselves by a factor of a million to a few microns in size with a powerful ritual to do so. While within the body, scale ranges in combat are relative to the size of the PCs. A Body environment is included in this book. Map of the Body enter exit through skin Antibodies are dispatched to neutralize foreign bodies. Antibodies mimic the PCs in an attempt to cancel out their threat. Bacteria Swarm Bacteria Swarms are present in the mouth and any infected parts of the body. Friendly Bacteria might be found in the intestines. The Wizard’s body was gross, but I never bothered learning how to teleport and we needed them alive to take us home. -	Eryn

name: Inner Demon
description: A demonic manifestation of dark inner thoughts.

features.name: Mental Game
features.description: The Demon has advantage against targets that have marked more Stress than the Demon.

features.name: Bitter Memories
features.description: Mark a Stress and choose a point within Close range. All targets within Very Close range of that point must make a Presence Reaction Roll. Targets who fail have their spirits weighed down with resurfaced painful memories and must decrease their Hope Die by one step (such as from d12 todlO) until they succeed with Hope or the Demon is defeated.

features.name: Possess
features.description: Spend a Fear to attempt to take control of a PC's body within Melee range. The target must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll or become Possessed by the Demon until they succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll to end the possession. Attacks made against the Possessed target damage the target, after which they can attempt a new Instinct Reaction Roll to end the possession. Spotlight the Possessed target instead of the Demon. Roll a d20 instead of Duality Dice to make an attack, and if you use an ability that costs Hope, spend Fear instead.

features.name: Guilt Trip
features.description: When the Demon takes Major or greater damage, the attacker must mark a Stress. Muscle Muscles are manifestations of the muscles in limbs. They take the form of the creature whose body they inhabit, but are composed entirely of muscle.
features.trigger: When the Demon takes Major or greater damage

name: Junior Seraph
description: A holy warrior and protector, leading a divine powerʼs forces into battle.

features.name: Winged Leader
features.description: Junior Seraph can fly and carry one ally up to a Close range as part of an action.

features.name: Many as One
features.description: Spend 2 Fearto activate1d4 allies within Far range. Attacks they make while spotlighted this way deal half damage.

features.name: Offer Alternatives
features.description: When a target within Close range rolls a failure with Hope,mark a Stress to offer them another solution to the challenge they face. If the target changes their mind and takes the offer, they gain 2 Hope.
features.trigger: When a target within Close range rolls a failure with Hope

features.name: Spare the Righteous
features.description: When an ally within Very Close range would mark HP,mark a Stressto allow them to mark 1 fewer HP.
features.trigger: When an ally within Very Close range would mark HP

name: Juvenile Flickerfly
description: Un insecte de la taille d’un cheval aux écailles iridescentes et aux ailes cristallines se déplaçant plus vite que l’œil ne peut suivre.

features.name: Relentless (3)
features.description: La Luciole peut être mise sous le projecteur jusqu’à trois fois par tour du MJ. Dépensez une Peur comme d’habitude pour la placer sous le projecteur.

features.name: Peerless Accuracy
features.description: Avant que la Luciole ne fasse une attaque, lancez un d6. Sur un résultat de 4 ou plus, l’Évasion de la cible est divisée par deux contre cette attaque.

features.name: Mind Dance
features.description: Créez un spectacle magiquement éblouissant qui emprisonne l’esprit des ennemis proches. Toutes les cibles à Portée Proche doivent faire un jet de Réaction d’Instinct. Pour chaque cible qui échoue, vous gagnez une Peur et la Luciole apprend une des peurs de la cible.

features.name: Hallucinatory Breath
features.description: Compte à rebours (Boucle 1d6). Quand la Luciole subit des dégâts pour la première fois, activez le compte à rebours. Quand il se déclenche, soufflez un gaz hallucinatoire sur toutes les cibles devant elle jusqu’à Portée Loin. Les cibles doivent réussir un jet de Réaction d’Instinct ou être tourmentées par des hallucinations terrifiantes. Les cibles dont les peurs sont connues ont un désavantage. Les cibles qui échouent doivent cocher un Stress et perdre un Espoir.
features.trigger: Quand la luciole subit des dégâts pour la première fois, activez le compte à rebours

name: Katari Bounty Hunter
description: A katari that tracks foes and hauls them in for ransom.

features.name: Chase
features.description: When the PCs are chasing the same bounty as the Katari, spend a Fear to summon the Katari. Set the Katari's countdown die to the same value as the PCs'. It ticks down when the bounty's die ticks down. If it triggers first or joint first with the bounty, the Katari catches the bounty and disappears with them.

features.name: Fly on the Wall
features.description: When a PC or group is discussing the whereabouts of the Katari's bounty, you can mark a Stress to reveal that the Katari is present in the scene, observing them. If the Katari escapes the scene to catch the bounty, you gain 2 Fear.

features.name: Entangle
features.description: During a chase, make an attack against a target if they would be within Far range. On a success, tick up the target's countdown die. Katari Pit Fighter Pit Fighters have the features of a large feline such as a jaguar and fight for gold. Katari Witch Witches are female Katari who hex their opponents. They can brew rare potions and poisons.

name: Kelp Forest
description: A patch of kelp floating gently in the water currents.

features.name: Plant
features.description: The Kelp Patch can't move. Horde (1d6+l): When the Kelp have marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 1d6+2 physical damage instead.

features.name: Algal Bloom
features.description: Spend a Fear to cover the area within Close range in a toxic cloud of algae that lasts until the end of the scene. All creatures within that area must mark a Stress when they act.

features.name: Magic Absorption
features.description: When the Kelp takes Major or lesser magic damage, you can mark a Stress to take no damage and instead grow in size, gaining 1 HP even if it would exceed their HP maximum.
features.trigger: When the Kelp takes Major or lesser magic damage

features.name: Sticky Hold
features.description: When a creature moves through the Kelp, you can mark a Stress to force them to make a Strength Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target is Restrained. Mandrake Thornbush Mandrakes are plants with root systems that resemble tiny humanoids. They attack unsuspecting victims in underground tunnels, often planted to guard passageways to reveal the presence of intruders through their screams. A Thornbush is a large, tangled mass of thorns found naturally in the wilds and might also be grown in manor grounds. Larger Thornbushes can completely block passage, such as a Thornbush hedge in a maze.
features.trigger: When a creature moves through the Kelp

name: Kelpie
description: A shapeshifting water spirit that haunts rivers and lochs, most often taking the form of a glistening black horse. To the unwary, it seems beautiful and harmless, but once touched its skin clings like glue, dragging the victim into the depths.

features.name: Fatal Embrace
features.description: Make a standard attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, mark a Stress to Restrain the target until they break free with a successful attack, Finesse Roll, or Strength Roll.

features.name: Drown Them
features.description: Spend a Fear to drown a target that is Restrained by Kelpie. The target takes 3d8+3 direct physical damage.

features.name: Luring Form
features.description: The Kelpie shifts shape into a human guise or a fine horse. All PCs within Close range must succeed on a Presence Reaction Roll or be Charmed. While Charmed a PC is unable to attack the Kelpie until it attacks them or an ally.

name: Knight of the Realm
description: Un soldat décoré en armure lourde avec une monture puissante.

features.name: Chevalier
features.description: Tant que le Chevalier est monté, il gagne un bonus de +2 à sa Difficulté. Quand il subit des dégâts Sévères, il est désarçonné et perd ce bonus jusqu’à sa prochaine mise sous le projecteur.

features.name: Heavily Armored
features.description: Quand le Chevalier subit des dégâts physiques, réduisez-les de 3.

features.name: Cavalry Charge
features.description: Si le Chevalier est monté, déplacez-vous jusqu’à Portée Loin et faites une attaque standard contre une cible. En cas de réussite, infligez 2d8+4 dégâts physiques et la cible doit cocher un Stress.

features.name: For the Realm!
features.description: Placez sous le projecteur 1d4+1 alliés. Les attaques qu’ils effectuent ainsi infligent la moitié des dégâts.

name: Krampus
description: A demonic bearded faun carrying a sack and rod.

features.name: Terrifying
features.description: When the Krampus makes a successful attack, all PCs within Very Close range lose a Hope and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Lumps of Coal
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against all targets in front of the Krampus within Far range. On a success, deal 1d8+2 physical damage and the target becomes temporarily Vulnerable from the Krampus learning why they've been naughty.

features.name: Kidnap
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range that the Krampus has learned has been naughty. On a success, the target is Restrained in an extradimensional sack carried by the Krampus. The target can break free with a successful Strength Roll (15) or is freed automatically if the Krampus takes Severe damage.

features.name: Retaliation
features.description: When the Krampus takes damage from an attack within Very Close range, you can mark a Stress to make a standard attack against the attacker. Faun ]anus A Janus is a monstrous two-headed humanoid formed by the magical fusion of two people. Two Fauns becoming a Janus leads to constant butting of the two heads. Krampus A Krampus is a fur-covered demonic Faun that appears in remote arctic regions before winter celebrations, kidnapping naughty children within a sack they carry across their back. Satyr Satyrs are woodland Fauns who need no excuse to throw a wild party.
features.trigger: When the Krampus takes damage from an attack within Very Close range

name: Lady of the Red Death
description: A masked lady dancing ecstatically to resist a deadly contagion, going berserk if unable to dance.

features.name: Curse of the Red Death
features.description: The "Red Death" Tier 2 Event is active, which includes the rules for the Red Death condition. If the Lady dies, the Red Death starts spreading from her body, activating the "Red Death Mist" Tier 2 Environment.

features.name: Infected Claws
features.description: Targets taking Major or greater damage from the Lady's claws make a Strength Reaction Roll (14) to avoid contracting Red Death (1).

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Lady can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight her.

features.name: Draining Dance
features.description: Countdown (Loop 3). While Dancing and not Restrained, the Lady feeds on the life force of nearby living creatures, activating this countdown. It ticks down when a PC takes an action. When it triggers, all targets within the ballroom (or within Far range while in the open) must make a Strength Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 2d10 direct magic damage and mark a Stress. If one or more targets fail, roll 1d6 + their amount. On a 6+, the Lady clears 1 HP and 1 Stress.
features.trigger: While Dancing, countdown triggers

features.name: Latent Threat
features.description: When a PC rolls a success against the Lady or somehow affects her, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: PC rolls success against the Lady

features.name: Plagued Body
features.description: If the Lady takes any damage that breaks her skin and isn't fire, the wound sprays infected red vapors. Living targets within Very Close range take 1d10 direct physical damage. Targets taking Major or greater damage make a Strength Reaction Roll (13) to avoid contracting Red Death (1).
features.trigger: Takes non-fire damage that breaks skin

features.name: Rapture Dance
features.description: Countdown (4). While hearing any kind of music, the Lady is Dancing and can't take any action. Tick the countdown down when the Lady marks HP, becomes Restrained, or is spotlighted after being harmed or made unable to dance. The countdown instantly triggers if the Lady can't hear any music. When it triggers, the Lady stops Dancing and clears 1d4 Stress. If the Lady hears music again, you can spend up to 8 Fear to reset the countdown to a number equal to half the Fear spent.
features.trigger: While hearing music

name: Laughing Lasher
description: A spine-covered dragon with tattered wings, found in forests and jungles tormenting their prey.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Lasher can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Flawed Wings
features.description: The Lasher can fly up to a Far range as part of their action.

features.name: Fly-By
features.description: Mark a Stressand draw a line to a point within Far range. The Lasher makes an attack against every target along that line. Targets the attack succeeds against must mark an armor slot without gaining its benefit (they may still use armor to reduce the damage).

features.name: Shred Magic
features.description: When the Lasher is the target of a spell, make a Reaction Roll (12). On a success,spend a Fearto force the spellcaster to mark a Stress or cancel the spell effect.
features.trigger: When the Lasher is the target of a spell

features.name: Momentum
features.description: When this adversary makes a successful attack against a PC, you gain a Fear.
features.trigger: When this adversary makes a successful attack against a PC

features.name: Deadly Serious (Phase Change)
features.description: When the Lasher marks their last HP, replace them with Snarling Sadist and immediately spotlight them. YOUNG THORN DRAGON Snarling sadist
features.trigger: When the Lasher marks their last HP

name: Leshy Lord
description: A centaur made from the bark and vine of the jungle with the exposed skull and antlers of an elk

features.name: Goring Charge
features.description: Mark a Stress to charge through a group within Close range and make an attack against all targets in the Leshy Lord’s path. Targets the Leshy Lord succeeds against take 2d8+3 physical damage and are knocked back to Far range. If a target is knocked into a solid object or another creature, they take an extra 1d6 damage (combine the damage).

features.name: Bramble Wall
features.description: Once per scene, Choose two points within Far range to create a wall of brambles. A PC attempting to climb over or through the wall must make a Finesse Roll. On a failure the PC is temporarily Restrained and takes 2d6 physical damage.

features.name: Thorn Barrage
features.description: Spend a Fear to spotlight two allies within Far range.

features.name: Guardian
features.description: When the Leshy Lord is within Very Close range of an ally who would take damage, mark a Stress to move into Melee range of them and take the damage instead then make a standard attack against the attacker.
features.trigger: When the Leshy Lord is within Very Close range of an ally who would take damage

name: Lightning Crab
description: A large crab buzzing with electrical energy.

features.name: Lightning Fast
features.description: The Crab can move up to Far range instead of Close range before taking an action.

features.name: Shock
features.description: Spend a Fear to make an attack against all targets touching the same body of water as the Crab up to Very Close range. Targets the Crab succeeds against take 2d10 magic damage, and must succeed on an Instinct Reaction Roll (11) or become temporarily Stunned. While Stunned, they can't act until they mark 2 Stress to end the condition.

features.name: Retaliatory Zap
features.description: When an attacker hits the Crab with a metal weapon or body part from Melee range, you can mark a Stress to deal IdlO magic damage to them. Elemental Hydra Hydras are monstrosities with multiple heads that can rapidly regrow a decapitated head. Elemental Hydras have up to four heads, each with different elemental capabilities. The elders of Kragg condemned criminals to death by I Hydra, an event the whole town gathered to watch. - Archibald
features.trigger: When an attacker hits the Crab with a metal weapon or body part from Melee range

name: Likho
description: A one-eyed, hunchbacked creature of ill omen, the Likho embodies pure misfortune. Those who cross its path face unnatural accidents, cruel luck, and an unshakable sense of impending doom.

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Likho can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Terrifying
features.description: When the Likho makes a successful attack, all PCs within Close range lose a Hope and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Weight of Misfortune
features.description: Spend a Fear to curse one creature within Close range with Misfortune until they roll a success with Hope or their next rest. While Misfortuned, the creature replaces their Hope die with a d8. When they roll a success with Fear, they lose a Hope. When they roll a failure with Fear, they lose a Hope and must mark a Stress. When they cannot lose a Hope as a result of this feature, they instead must mark a Hit Point.

features.name: Don't Trouble Trouble Until Trouble Troubles You
features.description: When a creature within Close range that is Misfortuned rolls a success with Fear, you can mark a Stress to spotlight the Likho without spending a Fear.

name: Lindwyrm
description: A lanky reptile with green scales and avian legs that crawls through the canopies, dropping down on unsuspecting prey.

features.name: Forest Climber
features.description: The Lindwyrm climbs just as easily as they run. When attacking from above, the Lindwyrm has advantage.

features.name: Savage Slam
features.description: Spend a Fear and target a group within Close range. All targets must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll. Targets who fail take 2d6+10 physical damage must mark a stress. All targets are knocked back to Far range.

features.name: Entangling Tail
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Close range. On a success, the target takes 2d8+3 physical damage and is Restrained. While Restrained the target moves with the Lindwyrm and each time the Lindwyrm takes an action, the target must mark a Stress. A target can break free with a successful Strength Roll.

name: Living Doll Horde
description: A small, broken-looking doll with features that resemble humanoids with an unswerving loyalty to their creator.

features.name: Horde (2d4+3)
features.description: When the Horde has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 2d4+3 physical damage instead.

features.name: Empty Vessel
features.description: Once per scene, when the Horde makes a successful attack, mark 1 HP and summon a Totem of Fear. Describe how the doll takes on traits of the target.
features.trigger: successful attack

name: Lizardfolk Chameleon
description: A lithe lizardfolk with color-changing skin.

features.name: Climber
features.description: The Lizardfolk climbs just as easily as they run.

features.name: Tongue Grab
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against a target within Close range. On a success, steal one item or consumable from the target's inventory.

features.name: Blend In
features.description: When the Lizardfolk makes a successful attack, you can mark a Stress to become Hidden until the Lizardfolk's next attack or a PC succeeds on an Instinct Roll (15) to find them.
features.trigger: When the Lizardfolk makes a successful attack

name: Lizardfolk Gila Monster
description: A large lizard folk with rough skin covered with war paint.

features.name: Ramp Up
features.description: You must spend a Fear to spotlight the Lizardfolk. While spotlighted, they can make their standard attack against all targets within range.

features.name: Reckless
features.description: The Lizardfolk makes attack rolls with advantage and attack rolls against them are made with advantage.

features.name: Grapple
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, the target is Restrained until they break free with a successful Strength Roll or the Lizardfolk takes Severe damage. While Restrained, the Lizardfolk's standard attacks are made using the target as a club, with the same range and damage, and also dealing half damage to the Restrained target on each success.

features.name: Poison Bath
features.description: When the Lizardfolk takes Severe damage, all creatures within Very Close range are bathed in their poisonous blood, taking 2d10 magic damage. Lizardfolk Chameleon Chameleons are masters of camouflage, hiding even when in plain sight. Lizardfolk Gila Monster Gila Monsters are monstrously large Lizardfolk with hard, rough skin and poisonous blood. Lizardfolk Monitor Monitors are trained skirmishers with thick muscular tails, who act as lookouts.
features.trigger: When the Lizardfolk takes Severe damage

name: Lizardfolk Monitor
description: A lizardfolk with a muscular tail, armed with a spear. Motives & Tactics: Keep watch, roam, block escape

features.name: Monitoring
features.description: You can mark a Stress to reveal the Lizardfolk hiding underwater or buried in sand. Immediately spotlight the Lizardfolk and make an attack with advantage.

features.name: Tail Slam
features.description: Mark a Stress and make an attack against a target within Melee range. On a success, deal 2d10 physical damage and the target is knocked back to Close range.

features.name: Tooth and Nail
features.description: When the Lizardfolk makes a standard attack with their spear beyond Very Close range, their standard attack becomes a Bite and Claw attack with Melee range. Use the same attack modifier but deal 2d6+2 physical damage on a success. Lummox A Lummox is a large, stupid, and clumsy creature with dark impulses, resembling a rage-fueled panda bear. It wanders from place to place, grabbing things that pleases its eye, indifferent to any damage or strife caused. When a Lummox finds a new settlement townsfolk are sometimes abducted in the night, and adventurers are hired to retrieve them.
features.trigger: When the Lizardfolk makes a standard attack with their spear beyond Very Close range

name: Lummox
description: A large panda-like creature with blood-streaked fur.

features.name: Padded
features.description: When the Lummox takes Minor damage, you can mark 2 Stress instead of a HP.

features.name: Wild Swing
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range with advantage. On a success, deal 2d12+6 physical damage and the target is knocked back to Far range. The Lummox becomes Vulnerable to the next attack made against them.

features.name: Grapple
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, the target is Restrained until they break free with a successful Strength Roll or the Lummox takes Severe damage. While Restrained, the Lummox's standard attacks are made using the target as a club, with the same range and damage, and also dealing half damage to the Restrained target on each success.

features.name: Destructive Rage
features.description: Spend a Fear to knock down a tree, wall or other item within Close range. Affected creatures must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll (15) or take 2d10 physical damage.

features.name: Mid-Battle Snack
features.description: Once per scene, clear 2 HP and 2 Stress. Lycans Lycans (or Lycanthropes) are humanoids afflicted with a curse that transforms them into a hybrid animal form. A Lycan ancestry is provided in this book.

name: Maddened Dwarf
description: A dwarf with crazed eyes, whispering to the rocks.

features.name: Spreading Madness
features.description: Targets who mark HP from the Dwarf's attacks must also mark a Stress. If they have marked all Stress, the target becomes Maddened until they clear all marked Stress. While Maddened they can only take short rest downtime moves during a long rest and they develop a flaw such as a compulsion, obsession, or phobia.

features.name: Darkness
features.description: Mark a Stress to extinguish all non-magical light sources up to Far range. Targets in darkness who can't see lose a Hope and have disadvantage on action rolls.

features.name: Cave-In
features.description: Spend a Fear while in an underground passageway to create a downfall of rocks up to Close range in front of the Dwarf. All targets must make an Agility Reaction Roll. On a failure, targets take 2d10 physical damage. On a success, targets take half damage. The passageway becomes blocked and can be cleared with a Progress Countdown (8). E Ekoko The Ekoko is a large, blind beast with bat-like wings that lives underground. It has two sensitive ear-like pits in its head in place of eyes, which gives them an hearing, and allows it to movement. The Ekoko can terrifying screech and use acute sense of detect nearby also let out a echolocation to map the surrounding environment and locate their prey.

name: Mandrake Knot
description: A group of mandrake roots tied together by their stems. They howl and scream, causing death in those that hear them.

features.name: Horde (2d6+2)
features.description: When the Mandrake Knot has marked half or more of their HP, their standard attack deals 2d6+2 physical damage instead.

features.name: Howl of Impending Doom
features.description: Spend a Fear to cause the Mandrake Knot to scream. All targets within Very Close range of the Mandrake Knot must succeed on a Strength or Instinct Reaction Roll or take 1d12 direct magic damage and start a Death Countdown (4). All countdowns tick down when any PC within the scene rolls with Fear. When it triggers, the target makes a death move. If the Mandrake Knot is defeated, all Death Countdowns end.

name: Manticore
description: A half-lion, half-dragon beast with a spiked tail.

features.name: Deadly Flight
features.description: While flying, the Manticore can move up to Far range instead of Close range before taking an action.

features.name: Terrifying
features.description: When the Manticore makes a successful attack, all PCs within Close range lose a Hope and you gain a Fear.

features.name: Spiked Tail
features.description: Make an attack with the Manticore's tail against a target within Very Close range. On a success the target takes 2d6+2 physical damage and gains a Spike token. A target with Spike tokens takes 1d6 physical damage per Spike token when they move. All Spike tokens can be removed by succeeding on a Finesse Roll (12 + the number of Spike tokens).

name: Masked Thief
description: Un voleur rusé doté de talents acrobatiques et d’un goût pour le spectaculaire.

features.name: Quick Hands
features.description: Faites une attaque contre une cible à Portée de Mêlée. En cas de réussite, infligez 1d8+2 dégâts physiques et le Voleur dérobe un objet ou consommable de l’inventaire de la cible.

features.name: Escape Plan
features.description: Révélez un piège à collet placé n’importe où sur le champ de bataille. Toutes les cibles à Portée Très Proche doivent réussir un jet de Réaction d’Agilité (13) ou être soulevées et suspendues la tête en bas. La cible est Entravée et Vulnérable jusqu’à ce qu’elle se libère avec un jet de Finesse ou de Force réussi (13).

name: Masquerade Party
description: A lavish party held in a noble's manor, with guests in ballgowns and faces hidden behind elaborate masks.

features.name: Hidden Faces
features.description: Recognizing a known face requires a successful Knowledge Roll. Who are the well known socialites and notorious scoundrels in this settlement? What masks do the PCs and NPCs wear?

features.name: Civil Society
features.description: No armor or weapons are allowed at the party. What are the PCs able to smuggle in without detection?

features.name: Gossip Circle
features.description: A group gathers nearby to talk about a topic of interest to the PCs. A PC can make a Presence Roll to join the conversation or a Finesse Roll to sneak up and eavesdrop. What is the discussion about? Who is behind the masks?

features.name: Dance
features.description: Music begins to play signaling that it is time to find a partner for a dance. What information can be learned during the dance? How comfortable are the PCs at dancing?

features.name: The Main Event
features.description: The head of the household makes a speech celebrating the cause of the gathering. Spend a Fear to spotlight an adversary who adds a complication, such as an assassination attempt. What was the purpose of the gathering - a marriage, coming of age ceremony, or for building political alliances?

features.name: Social Judgment
features.description: If a PC makes a social faux pas, they must mark a Stress. How will the PC make up for their error? Are they anonymous enough behind their mask?
features.trigger: If a PC makes a social faux pas

features.name: Insult
features.description: If a guest is insulted, spend a Fear to issue a challenge to a duel. When and where is the challenge? What weapons and rules will be decided?
features.trigger: If a guest is insulted

name: Master Assassin
description: Un tueur aguerri à la voix menaçante et à la lame mortelle.

features.name: Won't See It Coming
features.description: L’Assassin inflige des dégâts directs tant qu’il est Caché.

features.name: Strike as One
features.description: Placez sous le projecteur un nombre d’autres Assassins égal aux Stress non cochés de l’Assassin.

features.name: The Subtle Blade
features.description: Quand l’Assassin réussit une attaque standard contre une cible Vulnérable, infligez des dégâts Sévères au lieu des dégâts standard.
features.trigger: Quand l’assassin réussit une attaque standard contre une cible vulnérable

features.name: Momentum
features.description: Quand l’Assassin réussit une attaque contre un PJ, vous gagnez une Peur.
features.trigger: Quand l’assassin réussit une attaque contre un pj, vous gagnez une peur

name: Mecha Suit
description: A metallic suit worn by a person.

features.name: Sturdy Frame
features.description: The Suit is resistant to physical damage.

features.name: Kinetic Slam
features.description: Targets who take damage from the Suit's standard attacks are knocked back to Very Close range.

features.name: Overload
features.description: Before rolling damage for the Suit's standard attack, you can mark a Stress to gain a +10 bonus to the damage roll. The Suit can then take the spotlight again.

features.name: Power Down (Phase Change)
features.description: When the Suit marks their last HP, replace them with their occupant and immediately spotlight them. Mecha Suit Variants Some suits might have been built with additional capabilities: •	Jet Pack: Mark a Stress to move to Far range before taking an action roll. •	Missiles: Mark a Stress and make an attack against up to 3 targets within Close range. On a success, deal 2d8+2 physical damage. •	Mana Beam: Spend a Fear to release a beam of energy in a line up to Far range. All targets must succeed on an Agility Reaction Roll or take 2d6+5 magic damage. Merfolk Merfolk are humanoids that have piscine qualities including gills and a fish tail. They live in underwater settlements built close to the water’s surface from which they can trade with passing ships or raid fishing villages.
features.trigger: When the Suit marks their last HP

name: Merchant Baron
description: Un marchand accompli dirigeant une vaste opération commerciale.

features.name: Everyone Has a Price
features.description: Offrez à une cible un marché dangereux pour quelque chose qu’elle veut ou dont elle a besoin. Si utilisé sur un PJ, celui-ci doit faire un jet de Réaction de Présence (17). En cas d’échec, il doit cocher 2 Stress ou accepter le marché.

features.name: The Best Muscle Money Can Buy
features.description: Une fois par scène, invoquez 1d4+1 adversaires de Tier 1, qui apparaissent à Portée Loin, pour faire respecter la volonté du Baron.

name: Merfolk Aquamancer
description: A merfolk skilled in water magic.

features.name: Waterjet
features.description: The Merfolk can move up to Far range instead of Close range before taking an action.

features.name: Whirlpool
features.description: Mark a Stress to force a target in water within Close range to make an Agility Reaction Roll. On a failure, the target is Restrained within a whirlpool and has disadvantage on attack rolls until they break free with a successful Strength Roll.

features.name: Water Lasso
features.description: When the Merfolk makes a successful standard attack, you can spend a Fear to disarm the target of their primary weapon rather than deal damage. The Merfolk gains the target's weapon, which can be retrieved with a successful Finesse or Strength Roll (12) while within Melee range of the Merfolk. If the target doesn't have a secondary weapon equipped, they become Vulnerable until they rearm themselves. Merfolk Aquamancer Aquamancers can control water to help allies and hinder foes. Merfolk Harpoon er Harpooners fight with weapons suited to underwater combat.
features.trigger: When the Merfolk makes a successful standard attack

name: Merfolk Harpooner
description: A merfolk skilled in underwater combat.

features.name: Weighted Net
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Very Close range. On a success, the target is Restrained by a net until they break free with a successful Finesse Roll (12). If the target is in water they must mark a Stress as they begin to sink.

features.name: Reel
features.description: When the Merfolk makes a successful standard attack, you can mark a Stress to pull the target into Melee range of the Merfolk. Modified Modified (or Modded) are people that have artificially modified their bodies to enhance their abilities, whether through serums, implants, or prosthetics. The modifications enhance their natural abilities, and they can drain an implant’s charge to complete inhuman feats. Mentalist A Mentalist has enhanced their mental capacities, for instance through an implanted chip attached to their brain. They can rapidly calculate probabilities and act on them to gain an advantage. Supersoldier A Supersoldier has enhanced their limbs, granting inhuman strength and reflexes. This could be from an injected serum or prosthetics.
features.trigger: When the Merfolk makes a successful standard attack

name: Minor Nature Spirit
description: A mote of energy that takes the form of an animal.

features.name: Shape Nature
features.description: Mark a Stress to make the environment harder to move through. All creatures must mark a Stress to move beyond Very Close range until the end of the scene.

features.name: Group Attack
features.description: Spend a Fear to choose a target and spotlight all Spirits within Close range of them. Those Minions move into Melee range of the target and make one shared attack roll. On a success, they deal 5 physical damage each. Combine this damage.

name: Minotaur Wrecker
description: Un firbolg massif à tête de taureau avec un tempérament explosif.

features.name: Ramp Up
features.description: Vous devez dépenser une Peur pour placer le Minotaure sous le projecteur. Tant qu’il est sous le projecteur, il peut faire son attaque standard contre toutes les cibles à portée.

features.name: Charging Bull
features.description: Chargez à travers un groupe à Portée Proche et faites une attaque contre toutes les cibles sur le passage du Minotaure. Les cibles touchées subissent 2d6+8 dégâts physiques et sont repoussées à Portée Très Loin. Si une cible est projetée contre un objet solide ou une autre créature, elle subit 1d6 dégâts supplémentaires (combinez les dégâts).

features.name: Gore
features.description: Faites une attaque contre une cible à Portée Très Proche, en déplaçant le Minotaure à Portée de Mêlée d’elle. En cas de réussite, infligez 2d8 dégâts physiques directs.

name: Mire Worm
description: A giant centipede with hundreds of reaching arms and rows of teeth in its bisected belly maw

features.name: Relentless (2)
features.description: The Mire Worm can be spotlighted up to two times per GM turn. Spend Fear as usual to spotlight them.

features.name: Swamp Dweller
features.description: The Mire Worm is not impeded by deep water and can breathe underwater.

features.name: Watery Retreat
features.description: If the Mire Worm moves to a point that is underwater, all creatures it has Restrained start to drown unless they can breathe underwater or don’t require air. Any creature that is drowning has a Drowning Countdown (4). When the countdown triggers, the drowning creature makes a Death Move.

features.name: Entangling Assault
features.description: Mark a Stress to make an attack against all targets within Very Close range. Targets the Mire Worm succeeds against take 2d10+4 physical damage and are Restrained until they break free with a successful Strength or Finesse roll.

features.name: Flesh Prison
features.description: Make a standard attack with advantage against a target the Mire Worm has Restrained. On a success, the attack deals direct damage and the target is forced into the Mire Worm’s belly maw. A target Restrained in this way must spend a Hope or mark 1 HP to make an action roll and can make a Strength Roll to break free.

features.name: Body Blow
features.description: If the Mire Worm marks HP from an attack, spend a Fear to throw a creature Restrained by the Mire Worm at the attacker. The attacker must make an Agility Reaction Roll (17). On a failure, both the attacker and the creature take 2d10 physical damage. On a success, only the creature takes the damage. The creature thrown by the Mire Worm is no longer Restrained.
features.trigger: If the Mire Worm marks HP from an attack

name: Morph
description: A shapeshifting creature that can disguise themselves as humanoids.

features.name: Imitate
features.description: Before they make their first attack in a fight or after they shapeshift, the Morph is indistinguishable from a person they have disguised themselves as until they next act or a PC succeeds on an Instinct Roll to identify them.

features.name: Shapeshift
features.description: Once per scene, mark a Stress to change the Morph's appearance to a humanoid of any ancestry and immediately take the spotlight in that form. Choose a Tier 1 or Tier 2 adversary of that ancestry. You can use any of the features from that stat block in addition to the Morph's features.

features.name: Copycat
features.description: When a PC within Close range uses a spell or ability, you can mark a Stress to learn that spell or ability. When relevant while using the ability, spend or gain Fear instead of Hope. Morphling Morphlings are juvenile Morphs who can impersonate inanimate objects, waiting patiently to ambush unsuspecting victims.
features.trigger: When a PC within Close range uses a spell or ability

name: Morphling
description: A shapeshifting creature that can disguise themselves as objects.

features.name: Just an Object
features.description: Before they make their first attack in a fight or after they shapeshift, the Morph is indistinguishable from an object of the type they have disguised themselves as until they next act or a PC succeeds on an Instinct Roll to identify them.

features.name: Shapeshift
features.description: Mark a Stress to change into an inanimate object, becoming Hidden. Attacks made while Hidden in this way have advantage.

features.name: Tongue Lash
features.description: Make an attack against a target within Close range. On a success, the target takes 2d6+l physical damage and is pulled into Melee range.
